+    async def test_rejects_suspended_member(self):
+        account = _account()
+        with patch(
+            "app.middleware.org.account_org_repo.get_by_uid_and_org",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value=_membership(status="suspended")),
+        ):
+            with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
+                await require_active_org_member("org_a", account=account)
+        assert exc.value.status_code == 403
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_allows_personal_org_member(self):
+        account = _account()
+        with (
+            patch(
+                "app.middleware.org.account_org_repo.get_by_uid_and_org",
+                new=AsyncMock(return_value=_membership(role="admin", status="active")),
+            ),
+            patch(
+                "app.middleware.org.org_repo.get_by_id",
+                new=AsyncMock(return_value=_org(org_type="personal")),
+            ),
+        ):
+            result = await require_active_org_member("org_a", account=account)
+        assert isinstance(result, AccountOrg)
+        assert result.org_id == "org_a"
+

+import tarfile
+import zipfile
+from io import BytesIO
+
+import pytest
+
+from app.errors import DomainValidationError
+from app.services.pack_store.archive_service import validate_archive_upload, validate_uploaded_archive_reference
+
+
+def _manifest(*, name: str = "hr-helper", version: str = "1.0.0") -> str:
+    return f"name: {name}\nversion: {version}\n"
+
+
+def _valid_files() -> dict[str, bytes]:
+    return {
+        "agent-pack.yaml": _manifest().encode(),
+        "AGENTS.md": b"# Agents\n",
+        "SOUL.md": b"# Soul\n",
+        "IDENTITY.md": b"# Identity\n",
+        "extras/readme.md": b"ok\n",
+    }
+
+
+def _zip_archive(files: dict[str, bytes]) -> bytes:
+    out = BytesIO()
+    with zipfile.ZipFile(out, "w") as archive:
+        for name, data in files.items():
+            archive.writestr(name, data)
+    return out.getvalue()
+
+
+def _tar_archive(files: dict[str, bytes]) -> bytes:
+    out = BytesIO()
+    with tarfile.open(fileobj=out, mode="w:gz") as archive:
+        for name, data in files.items():
+            info = tarfile.TarInfo(name)
+            info.size = len(data)
+            archive.addfile(info, BytesIO(data))
+    return out.getvalue()
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_accepts_valid_zip():
+    data = _zip_archive(_valid_files())
+
+    result = validate_archive_upload(
+        filename="candidate.zip",
+        content_type="application/zip",
+        data=data,
+        expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+        expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+
+    assert result.archive_ext == ".zip"
+    assert result.manifest_name == "hr-helper"
+    assert result.manifest_version == "1.0.0"
+    assert result.size == len(data)
+    assert len(result.sha256) == 64
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_accepts_valid_tar_gz():
+    data = _tar_archive(_valid_files())
+
+    result = validate_archive_upload(
+        filename="candidate.tar.gz",
+        content_type="application/gzip",
+        data=data,
+        expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+        expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+
+    assert result.archive_ext == ".tar.gz"
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_rejects_tgz_to_match_worker_contract():
+    data = _tar_archive(_valid_files())
+
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_archive_upload(
+            filename="candidate.tgz",
+            content_type="application/gzip",
+            data=data,
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_unsupported"
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_rejects_missing_required_root_file():
+    files = _valid_files()
+    files.pop("SOUL.md")
+
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_archive_upload(
+            filename="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            data=_zip_archive(files),
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_layout_invalid"
+    assert exc_info.value.context == {"missing": ["SOUL.md"]}
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_rejects_forbidden_top_level_dirs():
+    files = _valid_files()
+    files["skills/unsafe.py"] = b"pass\n"
+
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_archive_upload(
+            filename="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            data=_zip_archive(files),
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_layout_invalid"
+    assert exc_info.value.context == {"forbidden": ["skills"]}
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_rejects_unsafe_paths():
+    files = _valid_files()
+    files["../agent-pack.yaml"] = b"name: bad\nversion: 1.0.0\n"
+
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_archive_upload(
+            filename="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            data=_zip_archive(files),
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_zip_unsafe"
+
+
+def test_validate_archive_upload_rejects_manifest_mismatch():
+    files = _valid_files()
+    files["agent-pack.yaml"] = _manifest(name="other", version="1.0.0").encode()
+
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_archive_upload(
+            filename="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            data=_zip_archive(files),
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_manifest_mismatch"
+
+
+def test_validate_uploaded_archive_reference_accepts_worker_key():
+    result = validate_uploaded_archive_reference(
+        org_id="org_a",
+        display_id="hr_helper",
+        archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+        archive_name="candidate.zip",
+        content_type="",
+        size=123,
+        sha256="A" * 64,
+        manifest_name="hr-helper",
+        manifest_version="1.0.0",
+        expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+        expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+
+    assert result.archive_key == "org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip"
+    assert result.archive_ext == ".zip"
+    assert result.content_type == "application/zip"
+    assert result.sha256 == "a" * 64
+
+
+def test_validate_uploaded_archive_reference_accepts_tar_gz_worker_key():
+    result = validate_uploaded_archive_reference(
+        org_id="org_a",
+        display_id="hr_helper",
+        archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.tar.gz",
+        archive_name="candidate.tar.gz",
+        content_type="application/gzip",
+        size=123,
+        sha256="b" * 64,
+        manifest_name="hr-helper",
+        manifest_version="1.0.0",
+        expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+        expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+
+    assert result.archive_ext == ".tar.gz"
+
+
+def test_validate_uploaded_archive_reference_rejects_key_scope_mismatch():
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_uploaded_archive_reference(
+            org_id="org_a",
+            display_id="hr_helper",
+            archive_key="org_b/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+            archive_name="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            size=123,
+            sha256="a" * 64,
+            manifest_name="hr-helper",
+            manifest_version="1.0.0",
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_key_scope_mismatch"
+
+
+def test_validate_uploaded_archive_reference_rejects_extension_mismatch():
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_uploaded_archive_reference(
+            org_id="org_a",
+            display_id="hr_helper",
+            archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+            archive_name="candidate.tar.gz",
+            content_type="application/gzip",
+            size=123,
+            sha256="a" * 64,
+            manifest_name="hr-helper",
+            manifest_version="1.0.0",
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_extension_mismatch"
+
+
+def test_validate_uploaded_archive_reference_rejects_manifest_mismatch():
+    with pytest.raises(DomainValidationError) as exc_info:
+        validate_uploaded_archive_reference(
+            org_id="org_a",
+            display_id="hr_helper",
+            archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+            archive_name="candidate.zip",
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            size=123,
+            sha256="a" * 64,
+            manifest_name="other",
+            manifest_version="1.0.0",
+            expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+            expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+        )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.code == "pack_test.archive_manifest_mismatch"

+from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
+from types import SimpleNamespace
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock
+
+import pytest
+
+from app.database import pack_asset_repo, pack_test_run_repo
+from app.database.collections import PACK_ASSET_COLLECTION, PACK_TEST_RUN_COLLECTION
+from app.schema.pack_test import PackAsset, PackTestRun
+
+
+def _now() -> datetime:
+    return datetime(2026, 6, 15, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)
+
+
+def _run_doc(**overrides):
+    data = {
+        "test_run_id": "ptr_1",
+        "org_id": "org_a",
+        "created_by_uid": "uid_author",
+        "pack_name": "worldcup-news",
+        "display_id": "worldcup-news",
+        "pack_version": "0.1.0",
+        "listing_metadata": {},
+        "quick_commands": [],
+        "smoke_scenarios": [],
+        "archive_tmp_key": "org_a/worldcup-news/archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_name": "archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_ext": ".tar.gz",
+        "archive_size": 123,
+        "archive_sha256": "a" * 64,
+        "archive_content_type": "application/gzip",
+        "status": "previewing",
+        "cleanup_after": _now() + timedelta(hours=24),
+        "created_at": _now(),
+        "updated_at": _now(),
+    }
+    data.update(overrides)
+    return data
+
+
+def _asset_doc(**overrides):
+    data = {
+        "asset_id": "asset_1",
+        "org_id": "org_a",
+        "pack_id": "pack_1",
+        "source_test_run_id": "ptr_1",
+        "storage_key": "org_a/worldcup-news/archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_name": "archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_ext": ".tar.gz",
+        "archive_size": 123,
+        "archive_sha256": "a" * 64,
+        "content_type": "application/gzip",
+        "status": "active",
+        "created_by_uid": "uid_author",
+        "created_at": _now(),
+        "deleted_at": None,
+    }
+    data.update(overrides)
+    return data
+
+
+class _FakeCursor:
+    def __init__(self, docs):
+        self.docs = docs
+        self.sort_args = None
+        self.limit_arg = None
+
+    def sort(self, *args):
+        self.sort_args = args
+        return self
+
+    def limit(self, limit):
+        self.limit_arg = limit
+        return self
+
+    async def to_list(self, *, length):
+        assert length == self.limit_arg
+        return self.docs
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_pack_test_run_repo_crud_queries(monkeypatch):
+    run_doc = _run_doc()
+    coll = MagicMock()
+    coll.update_one = AsyncMock(return_value=SimpleNamespace(modified_count=1))
+    coll.find_one_and_update = AsyncMock(return_value=run_doc)
+    fake_mongo = SimpleNamespace(
+        create=AsyncMock(),
+        read_one=AsyncMock(return_value=run_doc),
+        find_one_and_update=AsyncMock(return_value=run_doc),
+        db={PACK_TEST_RUN_COLLECTION: coll},
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(pack_test_run_repo, "mongo", fake_mongo)
+
+    await pack_test_run_repo.create(PackTestRun.model_validate(run_doc))
+    by_id = await pack_test_run_repo.get_by_id("org_a", "ptr_1")
+    any_org = await pack_test_run_repo.get_by_id_any_org("ptr_1")
+    modified = await pack_test_run_repo.update_fields("org_a", "ptr_1", {"status": "previewing"})
+    transitioned = await pack_test_run_repo.transition_status(
+        org_id="org_a",
+        test_run_id="ptr_1",
+        from_statuses={"asset_received", "created"},
+        to_status="bot_allocating",
+        fields={"updated_at": _now()},
+    )
+    await pack_test_run_repo.attach_submission("org_a", "ptr_1", "sub_1", _now())
+

+from datetime import UTC, datetime
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+from fastapi import BackgroundTasks
+from starlette.requests import Request
+
+from app.routes.enterprise.pack_test_runs import create_pack_test_run_handler
+from app.schema.account_org import AccountOrg
+from app.schema.pack_test import PackTestRunCreateRequest, PackTestRunCreateResponse, PackTestUploadedArchive
+from app.services.pack_store.archive_service import UploadedArchiveReference
+
+
+def _now() -> datetime:
+    return datetime(2026, 6, 9, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)
+
+
+def _membership() -> AccountOrg:
+    return AccountOrg(
+        uid="uid_author",
+        org_id="org_a",
+        role="admin",
+        status="active",
+        joined_at=_now(),
+    )
+
+
+def _validated_archive() -> UploadedArchiveReference:
+    return UploadedArchiveReference(
+        archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+        archive_name="candidate.zip",
+        archive_ext=".zip",
+        size=7,
+        sha256="b" * 64,
+        content_type="application/zip",
+        manifest_name="hr-helper",
+        manifest_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+
+
+def _request() -> PackTestRunCreateRequest:
+    return PackTestRunCreateRequest(
+        pack_name="hr-helper",
+        display_id="hr_helper",
+        pack_version="1.0.0",
+        archive=PackTestUploadedArchive(
+            key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+            name="candidate.zip",
+            size=7,
+            sha256="b" * 64,
+            content_type="application/zip",
+            manifest_name="hr-helper",
+            manifest_version="1.0.0",
+        ),
+        listing_metadata={"category": "HR"},
+        quick_commands=[{"title": "Start"}],
+        smoke_scenarios=[{"prompt": "hello"}],
+    )
+
+
+def _http_request(token: str = "creator-token") -> Request:
+    return Request(
+        {
+            "type": "http",
+            "method": "POST",
+            "path": "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs",
+            "headers": [(b"authorization", f"Bearer {token}".encode())],
+        }
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_create_pack_test_run_handler_uses_uploaded_archive_reference():
+    response = PackTestRunCreateResponse(
+        test_run_id="ptr_1",
+        org_id="org_a",
+        status="asset_received",
+        archive_sha256="b" * 64,
+        created_at=_now(),
+    )
+
+    with (
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.enterprise.pack_test_runs.validate_uploaded_archive_reference",
+            return_value=_validated_archive(),
+        ) as validate_reference,
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.enterprise.pack_test_runs.pack_test_service.create_test_run",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value=response),
+        ) as create_test_run,
+    ):
+        result = await create_pack_test_run_handler(
+            org_id="org_a",
+            request=_request(),
+            background_tasks=BackgroundTasks(),
+            http_request=_http_request(),
+            membership=_membership(),
+        )
+
+    assert result.test_run_id == "ptr_1"
+    validate_reference.assert_called_once_with(
+        org_id="org_a",
+        display_id="hr_helper",
+        archive_key="org_a/hr_helper/archive.zip",
+        archive_name="candidate.zip",
+        content_type="application/zip",
+        size=7,
+        sha256="b" * 64,
+        manifest_name="hr-helper",
+        manifest_version="1.0.0",
+        expected_pack_name="hr-helper",
+        expected_pack_version="1.0.0",
+    )
+    create_test_run.assert_awaited_once()
+    create_args = create_test_run.await_args
+    assert create_args is not None
+    assert create_args.kwargs["created_by_uid"] == "uid_author"
+    assert create_args.kwargs["archive"] == _validated_archive()
+    assert create_args.kwargs["listing_metadata"] == {"category": "HR"}
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_create_pack_test_run_handler_passes_creator_token_to_install_task():
+    response = PackTestRunCreateResponse(
+        test_run_id="ptr_1",
+        org_id="org_a",
+        status="asset_received",
+        archive_sha256="b" * 64,
+        created_at=_now(),
+    )
+    background_tasks = BackgroundTasks()
+
+    with (
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.enterprise.pack_test_runs.validate_uploaded_archive_reference",
+            return_value=_validated_archive(),
+        ),
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.enterprise.pack_test_runs.pack_test_service.create_test_run",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value=response),
+        ),
+    ):
+        await create_pack_test_run_handler(
+            org_id="org_a",
+            request=_request(),
+            background_tasks=background_tasks,
+            http_request=_http_request("creator-token-123"),
+            membership=_membership(),
+        )
+
+    assert len(background_tasks.tasks) == 1
+    task = background_tasks.tasks[0]
+    assert task.args == ("ptr_1",)
+    assert task.kwargs == {"archive_read_token": "creator-token-123"}

+from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
+from types import SimpleNamespace
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from app.schema.pack_test import PackTestRun
+from app.services.pack_store import pack_test_cleanup_service, pack_test_runtime_service
+
+
+def _now() -> datetime:
+    return datetime(2026, 6, 9, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)
+
+
+def _run(**overrides) -> PackTestRun:
+    data = {
+        "test_run_id": "ptr_1",
+        "org_id": "org_a",
+        "created_by_uid": "uid_author",
+        "pack_name": "Agent Studio",
+        "display_id": "agent_studio",
+        "pack_version": "1.0.0",
+        "listing_metadata": {},
+        "quick_commands": [],
+        "smoke_scenarios": [],
+        "archive_tmp_key": "org_a/agent_studio/archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_name": "candidate.tar.gz",
+        "archive_ext": ".tar.gz",
+        "archive_size": 123,
+        "archive_sha256": "a" * 64,
+        "archive_content_type": "application/gzip",
+        "status": "installing",
+        "cleanup_after": _now() + timedelta(hours=24),
+        "created_at": _now(),
+        "updated_at": _now(),
+    }
+    data.update(overrides)
+    return PackTestRun(**data)
+
+
+def test_billing_runtime_patch_updates_config_and_pod_env_keys():
+    config, deployment = pack_test_runtime_service._billing_runtime_patch(
+        "vk-build-user",
+        user_internal_token="builder-token",
+    )
+
+    assert config["models"]["providers"]["openai"]["apiKey"] == "vk-build-user"
+    assert config["agents"]["defaults"]["memorySearch"]["remote"]["apiKey"] == "vk-build-user"
+    assert deployment["env"]["LITELLM_API_KEY"] == "vk-build-user"
+    assert deployment["env"]["OPENAI_API_KEY"] == "vk-build-user"
+    assert deployment["env"]["USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN"] == "builder-token"
+
+
+def test_runtime_and_cleanup_helpers_cover_fallbacks():
+    assert (
+        pack_test_runtime_service._app_token_from_assets({"openclaw_app": {"api_token": " app-token "}}) == "app-token"
+    )
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._app_token_from_assets({"openclaw_app": "bad"}) == ""
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._main_mattermost_bot({"mattermost_bots": []}) is None
+    main_mm_bot = pack_test_runtime_service._main_mattermost_bot(
+        {"mattermost_bots": [{"agent_id": "side"}, {"agent_id": "main", "bot_user_id": "mm_bot"}]}
+    )
+    assert main_mm_bot is not None
+    assert main_mm_bot["bot_user_id"] == "mm_bot"
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._asset_bot_by_id({"openclaw_bots": "bad"}, "bot_1") is None
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._asset_bot_by_id({"openclaw_bots": [{"bot_id": "bot_1"}]}, "bot_1") == {
+        "bot_id": "bot_1"
+    }
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._deployment_ready({}) is True
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._deployment_ready({"deployment_status": {"status": ""}}) is True
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._deployment_ready({"deployment_status": {"status": "available"}}) is True
+    assert pack_test_runtime_service._deployment_ready({"deployment_status": {"status": "updating"}}) is False
+
+    truncated = pack_test_cleanup_service._failure_summary(RuntimeError("x" * 2500), max_chars=200)
+    assert "<truncated" in truncated
+    assert (
+        pack_test_cleanup_service._app_token_from_assets({"openclaw_app": {"api_token": " app-token "}}) == "app-token"
+    )
+    assert pack_test_cleanup_service._app_token_from_assets({"openclaw_app": "bad"}) == ""
+    assert pack_test_cleanup_service._as_utc(datetime(2026, 6, 15, 8, 0)).tzinfo == UTC
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_creator_billing_key_initializes_account_and_requires_key():
+    account = MagicMock()
+    account.model_dump.return_value = {"uid": "uid_author"}
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service.user_repo, "get_user", new=AsyncMock(return_value=account)),
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service,
+            "ensure_billing_initialized",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "uid_author", "billing_key": "vk-user"}),
+        ) as ensure_billing,
+    ):
+        assert await pack_test_runtime_service._creator_billing_key("uid_author") == "vk-user"
+
+    ensure_billing.assert_awaited_once_with({"uid": "uid_author"}, allow_terminal_subscription_bootstrap=True)
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service.user_repo, "get_user", new=AsyncMock(return_value=None)),
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service,
+            "ensure_billing_initialized",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "uid_author"}),
+        ),
+    ):
+        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="billing_key is missing"):
+            await pack_test_runtime_service._creator_billing_key("uid_author")
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_patch_bot_runtime_env_sends_config_and_deployment():
+    openclaw_client = MagicMock()
+    openclaw_client.update_bot_config = AsyncMock()
+
+    with patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "get_openclaw_client", return_value=openclaw_client):
+        await pack_test_runtime_service._patch_bot_runtime_env(

+    kwargs = openclaw_client.update_bot_config.await_args.kwargs
+    assert kwargs["app_token"] == "app-token"
+    assert kwargs["config"]["models"]["providers"]["openai"]["apiKey"] == "vk-user"
+    assert kwargs["deployment"]["env"]["USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN"] == "creator-token"
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_wait_for_bot_ready_after_patch_times_out_with_last_state():
+    openclaw_client = MagicMock()
+    openclaw_client.get_bot = AsyncMock(return_value={"deployment_status": {"status": "updating"}})
+    openclaw_client.get_bot_status = AsyncMock()
+
+    with patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "get_openclaw_client", return_value=openclaw_client):
+        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="updating"):
+            await pack_test_runtime_service._wait_for_bot_ready_after_patch(

+    openclaw_client.get_bot_status.assert_not_awaited()

+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_claim_temp_entry_claims_existing_or_newly_provisioned_entry():
+    entry = SimpleNamespace(uid="warm_uid")
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_repo,
+            "claim_one_ready",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value=entry),
+        ) as claim_one_ready,
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "mark_pool_unready", new=AsyncMock()) as mark_unready,
+        patch.object(

+
+    claim_one_ready.assert_awaited_once()
+    mark_unready.assert_awaited_once_with(uid_str="warm_uid", business="ecap")
+    upsert_assets.assert_awaited_once_with("warm_uid", {"asset_status": "claimed_for_pack_test"})
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_repo,
+            "claim_one_ready",
+            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=[None, entry]),
+        ) as claim_one_ready,
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service, "provision_one_for_immediate_claim", new=AsyncMock(return_value=object())
+        ) as provision,
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "mark_pool_unready", new=AsyncMock()),
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_assets_repo, "upsert_assets", new=AsyncMock()),
+    ):
+        assert await pack_test_runtime_service._claim_temp_entry() == entry
+
+    assert claim_one_ready.await_count == 2
+    provision.assert_awaited_once()
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_repo,
+            "claim_one_ready",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value=None),
+        ),
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service, "provision_one_for_immediate_claim", new=AsyncMock(return_value=None)
+        ) as provision,
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "mark_pool_unready", new=AsyncMock()) as mark_unready,
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_assets_repo, "upsert_assets", new=AsyncMock()
+        ) as upsert_assets,
+    ):
+        assert await pack_test_runtime_service._claim_temp_entry() is None

+    provision.assert_awaited_once()
+    mark_unready.assert_not_awaited()
+    upsert_assets.assert_not_awaited()

+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_claim_temp_runtime_discards_incomplete_entries_then_raises():

+async def test_install_test_run_deploys_uploaded_archive_with_official_pack_helper():
+    run = _run(status="asset_received")
+    bot = {

+        "mattermost_bots": [

+                "bot_user_id": "mm_bot",
+                "dm_channel_id": "dm_1",

+    assets = {
+        "app_token": "app-token",
+        "mattermost_user": {"user_id": "mm_user", "user_token": "mm-token"},
+        "openclaw_bots": [bot],
+    }
+    source = {
+        "archive_source_type": "remote_url",
+        "source_locator": "https://packs.example.com/org_a/agent_studio/archive.tar.gz",
+        "archive_name": "archive.tar.gz",
+    }
+    openclaw_client = MagicMock()
+    openclaw_client.restart_bot = AsyncMock()
+    call_order: list[str] = []
+
+    with (
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.pack_test_run_repo, "get_by_id_any_org", new=AsyncMock(return_value=run)
+        ),
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service.pack_test_run_repo,
+            "transition_status",
+            new=AsyncMock(
+                side_effect=[
+                    _run(status="bot_allocating"),
+                    _run(
+                        status="installing",
+                        temp_uid="warm_uid",
+                        temp_bot_id="bot_test",
+                        temp_computer_id="bot_test",
+                        temp_agent_id="main",
+                    ),
+                    _run(status="ready_for_preview"),
+                ]
+            ),
+        ) as transition_status,
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service, "_claim_temp_entry", new=AsyncMock(return_value=SimpleNamespace(uid="warm_uid"))
+        ),
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service.warm_pool_assets_repo, "get_assets", new=AsyncMock(return_value=assets)),
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "_creator_billing_key", new=AsyncMock(return_value="vk-build-user")),
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service,
+            "_patch_bot_runtime_env",
+            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda *args, **kwargs: call_order.append("patch")),
+        ) as patch_runtime_env,
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service,
+            "_wait_for_bot_ready_after_patch",
+            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda *args, **kwargs: call_order.append("wait")),
+        ) as wait_ready_after_patch,
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "_materialize_pack_test_runtime", new=AsyncMock()),
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "build_pack_archive_source", return_value=source) as build_source,
+        patch.object(
+            pack_test_runtime_service,
+            "deploy_official_agent_pack_archive",
+            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda *args, **kwargs: call_order.append("deploy")),
+        ) as deploy_archive,
+        patch.object(pack_test_runtime_service, "get_openclaw_client", return_value=openclaw_client),
+    ):
+        await pack_test_runtime_service.install_test_run("ptr_1", archive_read_token="creator-token")
+
+    build_source.assert_called_once_with("agent_studio", "org_a/agent_studio/archive.tar.gz")
+    patch_runtime_env.assert_awaited_once_with(
+        "bot_test",
+        "app-token",
+        "vk-build-user",
+        user_internal_token="creator-token",
+    )
+    wait_ready_after_patch.assert_awaited_once_with("bot_test", "app-token")
+    deploy_archive.assert_awaited_once_with(
+        "bot_test",
+        "app-token",
+        "main",
+        "/home/node/.openclaw/workspace-main",
+        source,
+        archive_read_token="creator-token",
+        overwrite_mode="clean",
+    )
+    assert call_order == ["patch", "wait", "deploy"]
+    assert transition_status.await_count == 3
+    openclaw_client.restart_bot.assert_awaited_once_with("bot_test", app_token="app-token")
+

+    return datetime(2026, 6, 9, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)

+            "app.services.pack_store.pack_test_service.computer_repo.update_fields",
+            new=AsyncMock(),
+        ) as update_computer,
+    ):

+import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
+
+import { createErrorResponse, proxyToBackend } from '@/lib/api/proxy'
+import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
+
+interface RouteContext {
+  params: Promise<{ computerId: string }>
+}
+
+/** GET /api/openclaw/computers/:computerId/pack-test-preview-session -> backend computer-scoped Pack Test session. */
+export async function GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: RouteContext) {
+  try {
+    const { computerId } = await params
+    const response = await proxyToBackend(
+      `/computers/${encodeURIComponent(computerId)}/pack-test-preview-session`,
+      undefined,
+      { method: 'GET', originalRequest: request },
+    )
+    if (!response.ok) {
+      const errorData = (await response.json().catch(() => ({}))) as { detail?: string }
+      logger.error(
+        '[API /openclaw/computers/:computerId/pack-test-preview-session] Backend error:',
+        response.status,
+        errorData,
+      )
+      return createErrorResponse(errorData.detail || `Backend returned ${response.status}`, response.status)
+    }
+    return NextResponse.json(await response.json())
+  } catch (error: unknown) {
+    logger.error('[API /openclaw/computers/:computerId/pack-test-preview-session] GET error:', error)
+    return createErrorResponse(error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error', 500)
+  }
+}

+import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
+
+import { createErrorResponse, proxyToBackend } from '@/lib/api/proxy'
+import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
+
+export async function POST(
+  request: NextRequest,
+  { params }: { params: Promise<{ orgId: string; testRunId: string }> },
+) {
+  try {
+    const { orgId, testRunId } = await params
+    const body = await request.json().catch(() => ({}))
+    const response = await proxyToBackend(
+      `/orgs/${encodeURIComponent(orgId)}/pack-test-runs/${encodeURIComponent(testRunId)}/accept`,
+      body,
+      {
+        method: 'POST',
+        timeout: 15000,
+        originalRequest: request,
+      },
+    )
+    const data = await response.json().catch(() => ({}))
+    return NextResponse.json(data, { status: response.status })
+  } catch (error: unknown) {
+    logger.error('[API /orgs/:orgId/pack-test-runs/:testRunId/accept] POST error:', error)
+    return createErrorResponse(error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error', 500)
+  }
+}

+import type { OpenClawComputer } from '@/lib/api/openclaw'
+
+const PREVIEWABLE_PACK_TEST_STATUSES = new Set(['ready_for_preview', 'previewing', 'accepted'])
+
+export function filterPackTestComputers(computers: OpenClawComputer[] | undefined): OpenClawComputer[] {
+  return (computers ?? []).filter(
+    (computer) =>
+      computer.source === 'pack_test' && PREVIEWABLE_PACK_TEST_STATUSES.has(computer.pack_test_status ?? ''),
+  )
+}

+'use client'
+
+import { useMutation, useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
+import { useMemo } from 'react'
+
+import {
+  acceptPackTestRun,
+  extendPackTestPreview,
+  getPackTestPreviewSession,
+  getPackTestRun,
+  type PackTestRun,
+} from '@/lib/api/pack-test'
+import { QUERY_VERSION } from '@/lib/query/keys'
+
+function unwrapPackTestRun(
+  result: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof acceptPackTestRun>>,
+  fallbackMessage: string,
+): PackTestRun {
+  if (!result.success || !result.data) throw new Error(result.error ?? fallbackMessage)
+  return result.data
+}
+
+const TERMINAL_STATUSES = new Set<PackTestRun['status']>([
+  'ready_for_preview',
+  'previewing',
+  'accepted',
+  'promoted',
+  'cleanup_pending',
+  'cleaned',
+  'failed',
+  'timed_out',
+])
+
+function canLoadPreview(status: PackTestRun['status'] | undefined): boolean {
+  return status === 'ready_for_preview' || status === 'previewing' || status === 'accepted'
+}
+
+export function usePackTestPreview(orgId: string | null, testRunId: string | null | undefined) {
+  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
+
+  const keys = useMemo(
+    () => ({
+      run: [QUERY_VERSION, 'pack-test', 'run', orgId, testRunId] as const,
+      preview: [QUERY_VERSION, 'pack-test', 'preview-session', orgId, testRunId] as const,
+    }),
+    [orgId, testRunId],
+  )
+
+  const runQuery = useQuery({
+    queryKey: keys.run,
+    enabled: !!orgId && !!testRunId,
+    queryFn: async ({ signal }) => {
+      const result = await getPackTestRun(orgId!, testRunId!, signal)
+      if (!result.success || !result.data) throw new Error(result.error ?? 'Failed to load pack test run')
+      return result.data
+    },
+    refetchInterval: (query) => {
+      const run = query.state.data
+      if (!run || !TERMINAL_STATUSES.has(run.status)) return 3000
+      return false
+    },
+  })
+
+  const previewQuery = useQuery({
+    queryKey: keys.preview,
+    enabled: !!orgId && !!testRunId && canLoadPreview(runQuery.data?.status),
+    queryFn: async ({ signal }) => {
+      const result = await getPackTestPreviewSession(orgId!, testRunId!, signal)
+      if (!result.success || !result.data) throw new Error(result.error ?? 'Failed to load preview session')
+      return result.data
+    },
+    staleTime: 60_000,
+    retry: 1,
+  })
+
+  const invalidate = () => {
+    void queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: keys.run })
+    void queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: keys.preview })
+  }
+
+  const acceptMutation = useMutation({
+    mutationFn: async () =>
+      unwrapPackTestRun(await acceptPackTestRun(orgId!, testRunId!), 'Failed to accept Pack Test'),
+    onSuccess: invalidate,
+  })
+
+  const extendMutation = useMutation({
+    mutationFn: async () =>
+      unwrapPackTestRun(await extendPackTestPreview(orgId!, testRunId!), 'Failed to extend Pack Test preview'),
+    onSuccess: invalidate,
+  })
+
+  return {
+    runQuery,
+    previewQuery,
+    acceptMutation,
+    extendMutation,
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+import { getAPI, postAPI } from './backend'
+
+export type PackTestRunStatus =
+  | 'created'
+  | 'asset_received'
+  | 'bot_allocating'
+  | 'installing'
+  | 'ready_for_preview'
+  | 'previewing'
+  | 'accepted'
+  | 'promoted'
+  | 'cleanup_pending'
+  | 'cleaned'
+  | 'failed'
+  | 'timed_out'
+
+export interface PackTestRun {
+  test_run_id: string
+  org_id: string
+  created_by_uid: string
+  pack_name: string
+  display_id: string
+  pack_version: string
+  archive_name: string
+  archive_size: number
+  archive_sha256: string
+  status: PackTestRunStatus
+  failure_code?: string | null
+  failure_summary?: string | null
+  preview_url?: string | null
+  preview_expires_at?: string | null
+  preview_extended_count: number
+  accepted_at?: string | null
+  promoted_asset_id?: string | null
+  submission_id?: string | null
+  created_at: string
+  updated_at: string
+}
+
+export interface PackTestPreviewSession {
+  test_run_id: string
+  token: string
+  user_id: string
+  channel_id: string
+  agent_id: string
+  bot_user_id: string
+  bot_name: string
+  expires_at: string
+}
+
+export function getPackTestRun(orgId: string, testRunId: string, signal?: AbortSignal) {
+  return getAPI<PackTestRun>(
+    `/api/orgs/${encodeURIComponent(orgId)}/pack-test-runs/${encodeURIComponent(testRunId)}`,
+    undefined,
+    signal,
+  )
+}
+
+export function getPackTestPreviewSession(orgId: string, testRunId: string, signal?: AbortSignal) {
+  return getAPI<PackTestPreviewSession>(
+    `/api/orgs/${encodeURIComponent(orgId)}/pack-test-runs/${encodeURIComponent(testRunId)}/preview-session`,
+    undefined,
+    signal,
+  )
+}
+
+export function acceptPackTestRun(orgId: string, testRunId: string, note?: string) {
+  return postAPI<PackTestRun>(
+    `/api/orgs/${encodeURIComponent(orgId)}/pack-test-runs/${encodeURIComponent(testRunId)}/accept`,
+    { note },
+  )
+}
+
+export function extendPackTestPreview(orgId: string, testRunId: string) {
+  return postAPI<PackTestRun>(
+    `/api/orgs/${encodeURIComponent(orgId)}/pack-test-runs/${encodeURIComponent(testRunId)}/extend-preview`,
+    {},
+  )
+}

author: Nemo Feng
title: fix(web): capture GA4 session source for sign-up attribution (#2449)
message:
fix(web): capture GA4 session source for sign-up attribution (#2449)

## Problem

~25% of GA4 sign-up conversions report a `Unassigned` / `(not set)`
traffic source. A GA4 exploration of `Unassigned` sessions (last ~4
weeks, prod) showed **~79% have `Session source/medium = (not set)`**,
concentrated on logged-in app pages — `/chat` (327), `/new-chat` (76),
`/user/verify`, `/claw-settings`, `/agents-manager`, plus
external-return pages like `/en/subscription/success` and
`/en/integrations/connector?status=success`. These are real,
deeply-engaged sessions (avg engagement 300s+), not bots.

GA4 builds `session_start` from whatever source the session's **first
event** carries. If that event arrives with no source, the session
resolves to `(not set)` — and since the `Direct` channel requires source
*exactly* `(direct)`, a blank source falls through to `Unassigned`.
Sign-up conversions fire on these same pages and inherit it.

## Root cause (two collection gaps)

1. **gtag init ran too late.** The `dataLayer`/`gtag` stub + `config`
lived in an `afterInteractive` `<Script>` (`TrackingScripts`). Events
fired before it loaded hit the `if (!w?.gtag) return` drop, and `config`
wasn't guaranteed to precede the first event — so the session's first
event could be recorded with no source. (This is the exact "events
before config" / "events dissociated from session" failure in GA4's own
[tagging best
practices](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/14847402).)
2. **The first `page_view` was delayed 1000ms** — a
Firebase-Analytics-era performance guard carried over verbatim from the
gensmo-web extraction. A user who taps a mobile-OAuth button or
otherwise navigates away within that 1s never records a landing source,
so `session_start` resolves to `(not set)`.

## Changes

- **New `src/lib/gtag-bootstrap.ts`**, inlined into
`src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` `<head>`: defines the `dataLayer`/`gtag`
queue and runs `config` **before hydration**. Early events now buffer in
`dataLayer` and replay in order *after* `config`, so `session_start`
always has a source. Preserves the verbatim `function
gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments)}` form (an arrow + rest-params pushes
Arrays that gtag.js ignores — regression fixed in #1133) and
`send_page_view: false`.
- **`TrackingScripts`** now loads only the gtag.js library
(`afterInteractive`, which drains the queue) + the Reddit pixel.
`GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` is now a single source of truth in
`gtag-bootstrap.ts`.
- **`usePageTracking`** fires the first `page_view` synchronously (the
effect already runs post-paint; with the `<head>` bootstrap a page_view
is a cheap `dataLayer.push`). Subsequent route changes stay deferred to
idle time.

## Test plan

- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh <changed paths>` — governance guards +
`tsc` + **65 vitest** + eslint, all green.
- Updated `usePageTracking` + `TrackingScripts` specs for the new
behavior; added `gtag-bootstrap` spec including a #1133 regression guard
on the verbatim gtag form.

## How to validate after deploy (staging DebugView)

Reproduce the dominant path — returning user opening `/chat` directly,
plus one mobile-Google and one email-link sign-up — and confirm
`session_start` now carries a real source instead of `(not set)`.

## Out of scope (separate, non-code follow-ups)

The investigation also surfaced GA4-side fixes that don't belong in this
PR: a Custom Channel Group mapping `medium=newsletter` → Email
(third-party newsletters we don't control:
joinhorizon/superhuman/beehiiv/aifire) and `source set + medium (not
set)` → Referral (novatools.cn, chatgpt.com); adding the Firebase auth /
Stripe / connector domains to the unwanted-referral list; and an
internal-traffic filter for `codex_test/debug`.

## Problem

~25% of GA4 sign-up conversions report a `Unassigned` / `(not set)` traffic source. A GA4 exploration of `Unassigned` sessions (last ~4 weeks, prod) showed **~79% have `Session source/medium = (not set)`**, concentrated on logged-in app pages — `/chat` (327), `/new-chat` (76), `/user/verify`, `/claw-settings`, `/agents-manager`, plus external-return pages like `/en/subscription/success` and `/en/integrations/connector?status=success`. These are real, deeply-engaged sessions (avg engagement 300s+), not bots.

GA4 builds `session_start` from whatever source the session's **first event** carries. If that event arrives with no source, the session resolves to `(not set)` — and since the `Direct` channel requires source *exactly* `(direct)`, a blank source falls through to `Unassigned`. Sign-up conversions fire on these same pages and inherit it.

## Root cause (two collection gaps)

1. **gtag init ran too late.** The `dataLayer`/`gtag` stub + `config` lived in an `afterInteractive` `<Script>` (`TrackingScripts`). Events fired before it loaded hit the `if (!w?.gtag) return` drop, and `config` wasn't guaranteed to precede the first event — so the session's first event could be recorded with no source. (This is the exact "events before config" / "events dissociated from session" failure in GA4's own [tagging best practices](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/14847402).)
2. **The first `page_view` was delayed 1000ms** — a Firebase-Analytics-era performance guard carried over verbatim from the gensmo-web extraction. A user who taps a mobile-OAuth button or otherwise navigates away within that 1s never records a landing source, so `session_start` resolves to `(not set)`.

## Changes

- **New `src/lib/gtag-bootstrap.ts`**, inlined into `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` `<head>`: defines the `dataLayer`/`gtag` queue and runs `config` **before hydration**. Early events now buffer in `dataLayer` and replay in order *after* `config`, so `session_start` always has a source. Preserves the verbatim `function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments)}` form (an arrow + rest-params pushes Arrays that gtag.js ignores — regression fixed in #1133) and `send_page_view: false`.
- **`TrackingScripts`** now loads only the gtag.js library (`afterInteractive`, which drains the queue) + the Reddit pixel. `GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` is now a single source of truth in `gtag-bootstrap.ts`.
- **`usePageTracking`** fires the first `page_view` synchronously (the effect already runs post-paint; with the `<head>` bootstrap a page_view is a cheap `dataLayer.push`). Subsequent route changes stay deferred to idle time.

## Test plan

- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh <changed paths>` — governance guards + `tsc` + **65 vitest** + eslint, all green.
- Updated `usePageTracking` + `TrackingScripts` specs for the new behavior; added `gtag-bootstrap` spec including a #1133 regression guard on the verbatim gtag form.

## How to validate after deploy (staging DebugView)

Reproduce the dominant path — returning user opening `/chat` directly, plus one mobile-Google and one email-link sign-up — and confirm `session_start` now carries a real source instead of `(not set)`.

## Out of scope (separate, non-code follow-ups)

The investigation also surfaced GA4-side fixes that don't belong in this PR: a Custom Channel Group mapping `medium=newsletter` → Email (third-party newsletters we don't control: joinhorizon/superhuman/beehiiv/aifire) and `source set + medium (not set)` → Referral (novatools.cn, chatgpt.com); adding the Firebase auth / Stripe / connector domains to the unwanted-referral list; and an internal-traffic filter for `codex_test/debug`.

commit.message:
fix(web): capture GA4 session source for sign-up attribution (#2449)

## Problem

~25% of GA4 sign-up conversions report a `Unassigned` / `(not set)`
traffic source. A GA4 exploration of `Unassigned` sessions (last ~4
weeks, prod) showed **~79% have `Session source/medium = (not set)`**,
concentrated on logged-in app pages — `/chat` (327), `/new-chat` (76),
`/user/verify`, `/claw-settings`, `/agents-manager`, plus
external-return pages like `/en/subscription/success` and
`/en/integrations/connector?status=success`. These are real,
deeply-engaged sessions (avg engagement 300s+), not bots.

GA4 builds `session_start` from whatever source the session's **first
event** carries. If that event arrives with no source, the session
resolves to `(not set)` — and since the `Direct` channel requires source
*exactly* `(direct)`, a blank source falls through to `Unassigned`.
Sign-up conversions fire on these same pages and inherit it.

## Root cause (two collection gaps)

1. **gtag init ran too late.** The `dataLayer`/`gtag` stub + `config`
lived in an `afterInteractive` `<Script>` (`TrackingScripts`). Events
fired before it loaded hit the `if (!w?.gtag) return` drop, and `config`
wasn't guaranteed to precede the first event — so the session's first
event could be recorded with no source. (This is the exact "events
before config" / "events dissociated from session" failure in GA4's own
[tagging best
practices](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/14847402).)
2. **The first `page_view` was delayed 1000ms** — a
Firebase-Analytics-era performance guard carried over verbatim from the
gensmo-web extraction. A user who taps a mobile-OAuth button or
otherwise navigates away within that 1s never records a landing source,
so `session_start` resolves to `(not set)`.

## Changes

- **New `src/lib/gtag-bootstrap.ts`**, inlined into
`src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` `<head>`: defines the `dataLayer`/`gtag`
queue and runs `config` **before hydration**. Early events now buffer in
`dataLayer` and replay in order *after* `config`, so `session_start`
always has a source. Preserves the verbatim `function
gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments)}` form (an arrow + rest-params pushes
Arrays that gtag.js ignores — regression fixed in #1133) and
`send_page_view: false`.
- **`TrackingScripts`** now loads only the gtag.js library
(`afterInteractive`, which drains the queue) + the Reddit pixel.
`GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` is now a single source of truth in
`gtag-bootstrap.ts`.
- **`usePageTracking`** fires the first `page_view` synchronously (the
effect already runs post-paint; with the `<head>` bootstrap a page_view
is a cheap `dataLayer.push`). Subsequent route changes stay deferred to
idle time.

## Test plan

- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh <changed paths>` — governance guards +
`tsc` + **65 vitest** + eslint, all green.
- Updated `usePageTracking` + `TrackingScripts` specs for the new
behavior; added `gtag-bootstrap` spec including a #1133 regression guard
on the verbatim gtag form.

## How to validate after deploy (staging DebugView)

Reproduce the dominant path — returning user opening `/chat` directly,
plus one mobile-Google and one email-link sign-up — and confirm
`session_start` now carries a real source instead of `(not set)`.

## Out of scope (separate, non-code follow-ups)

The investigation also surfaced GA4-side fixes that don't belong in this
PR: a Custom Channel Group mapping `medium=newsletter` → Email
(third-party newsletters we don't control:
joinhorizon/superhuman/beehiiv/aifire) and `source set + medium (not
set)` → Referral (novatools.cn, chatgpt.com); adding the Firebase auth /
Stripe / connector domains to the unwanted-referral list; and an
internal-traffic filter for `codex_test/debug`.

files.patch:
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'
 import { getBrandThemeBootstrapScript } from '@/app/_brand-bootstrap'
 import { getStructuredDataSchemas } from '@/app/_seo'
 import ClientLayout from '@/components/ClientLayout'
+import { getGtagBootstrapScript } from '@/lib/gtag-bootstrap'
 import { i18nConfig, isValidLocale, type Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/config'
 import { getActiveBrandAssets, resolveBrandThemeFromEnv } from '@/theme/brand-assets'
 
@@ -56,6 +57,13 @@ export default async function LocaleLayout({ children, params }: LocaleLayoutPro
           }}
         />
 
+        {/* Google tag (gtag.js) bootstrap — define the dataLayer/gtag queue and
+            run `config` BEFORE hydration so early events buffer instead of being
+            dropped, and session_start is attributed to a real traffic source
+            (not "(not set)" → Unassigned). The gtag.js library loads
+            afterInteractive via <TrackingScripts/> and replays the queue. */}
+        <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: getGtagBootstrapScript() }} />
+
         {/* Web App Manifest */}
         <link rel="manifest" href={activeBrandAssets.manifestUrl} />

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+/**
+ * Google tag (gtag.js) SSR bootstrap.
+ *
+ * `getGtagBootstrapScript()` returns Google's verbatim gtag.js init snippet to
+ * be inlined into the `<head>` of the html-bearing layout
+ * (`src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`) so it runs BEFORE hydration. Running it early
+ * — rather than from an `afterInteractive` <Script> in `TrackingScripts` —
+ * guarantees the `dataLayer`/`gtag` queue and the `config` command exist before
+ * any React code fires an event. Two consequences:
+ *   1. early events buffer in `dataLayer` instead of being dropped, and
+ *   2. GA4 builds `session_start` AFTER `config`, so the session is attributed
+ *      to a real traffic source instead of landing in "(not set)" → Unassigned.
+ *
+ * The gtag.js library itself still loads `afterInteractive` (in
+ * `components/TrackingScripts`); it replays the queued commands in order on load.
+ *
+ * `send_page_view: false` is intentional — page_view is fired manually on route
+ * change by `usePageTracking` to avoid SPA double-counting.
+ *
+ * Lives in `lib/` (not `app/`) because both the server layout and the client
+ * `TrackingScripts` import from it, so it must sit in a layer both can reach.
+ */
+
+const isProduction = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV === 'production'
+
+// production / staging report into separate GA4 properties.
+export const GA_MEASUREMENT_ID = isProduction ? 'G-MRK8Q360DH' : 'G-R5KDFHVTGK'
+
+// Google Ads conversion tag — production only.
+const GOOGLE_ADS_ID = 'AW-18078707186'
+
+export function getGtagBootstrapScript(): string {
+  // Verbatim Google form: a real `function(){dataLayer.push(arguments)}`.
+  // Do NOT "modernize" this into an arrow with rest params
+  // (`(...args) => dataLayer.push(args)`) — that pushes Arrays, which gtag.js
+  // ignores via Array.isArray(), silently dropping every command (see #1133).
+  return [
+    'window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];',
+    'function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}',
+    "gtag('js', new Date());",
+    `gtag('config', '${GA_MEASUREMENT_ID}', { send_page_view: false });`,
+    isProduction ? `gtag('config', '${GOOGLE_ADS_ID}');` : '',
+  ]
+    .filter(Boolean)
+    .join('\n')
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+describe('lib/gtag-bootstrap', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    // GA_MEASUREMENT_ID + the script are computed from NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV at
+    // module load, so re-import the module fresh under each stubbed env.
+    vi.resetModules()
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    vi.unstubAllEnvs()
+  })
+
+  it('emits the verbatim gtag function form (not an arrow that pushes Arrays)', async () => {
+    vi.stubEnv('NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV', 'production')
+    const { getGtagBootstrapScript } = await import('@/lib/gtag-bootstrap')
+    const script = getGtagBootstrapScript()
+
+    // Regression guard for #1133: gtag.js ignores Arrays, so the queue function
+    // must be a real `function(){dataLayer.push(arguments)}`.
+    expect(script).toContain('window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];')
+    expect(script).toContain('function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}')
+    expect(script).toContain("gtag('js', new Date());")
+  })
+
+  it('configures the production GA4 property with send_page_view disabled', async () => {
+    vi.stubEnv('NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV', 'production')
+    const { getGtagBootstrapScript, GA_MEASUREMENT_ID } = await import('@/lib/gtag-bootstrap')
+
+    expect(GA_MEASUREMENT_ID).toBe('G-MRK8Q360DH')
+    expect(getGtagBootstrapScript()).toContain("gtag('config', 'G-MRK8Q360DH', { send_page_view: false });")
+  })
+
+  it('includes the Google Ads tag in production', async () => {
+    vi.stubEnv('NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV', 'production')
+    const { getGtagBootstrapScript } = await import('@/lib/gtag-bootstrap')
+
+    expect(getGtagBootstrapScript()).toContain("gtag('config', 'AW-18078707186');")
+  })
+
+  it('uses the staging GA4 property and omits Google Ads outside production', async () => {
+    vi.stubEnv('NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV', 'staging')
+    const { getGtagBootstrapScript, GA_MEASUREMENT_ID } = await import('@/lib/gtag-bootstrap')
+    const script = getGtagBootstrapScript()
+
+    expect(GA_MEASUREMENT_ID).toBe('G-R5KDFHVTGK')
+    expect(script).toContain("gtag('config', 'G-R5KDFHVTGK', { send_page_view: false });")
+    expect(script).not.toContain('AW-18078707186')
+  })
+})

author: Chris@ZooClaw
title: fix(web): record Sentry latency metrics (#2446)
message:
fix(web): record Sentry latency metrics (#2446)

## Summary

- Move chat latency and connection duration numeric values from Sentry
Logs attributes to Sentry distribution metrics.
- Keep Logs for discrete event counts and dimensions only, so
count/user/grouping dashboards continue to work without duplicating
duration payloads.
- Update unit tests to assert latency/duration values are emitted
through `Sentry.metrics.distribution(...)` and no longer duplicated in
log attributes.

## Historical Sentry check

I checked the existing Sentry Logs data before changing the reporting
shape:

- Raw/API queries return `latency_ms`, `degraded_duration_ms`, and
`episode_duration_ms` as `null` / `string` fields, so Sentry cannot
compute historical p50/p95 from those fields.
- 14d historical count data is still available:
- `chat.message.response_latency`: `ack=380`, `reply_content=309`,
`reply_timeout=42`
  - reply-content route split: `chat=199`, `session-thread=110`
  - `chat.connection.status_mismatch`: `platform_not_ready=4`
  - display episode endings: `recovered=317`, `unmounted=58`
- `mm.connection.transition`: `connected=827`, `reconnecting=561`,
`disconnected=4`

## Dashboard state

The two Sentry dashboards have been updated directly:

- Chat Response Latency: percentile widgets now use `tracemetrics` /
distribution metric queries.
- OpenClaw Connection Status Mismatch: duration percentile widgets now
use `tracemetrics` categorical bars.
- Fresh dashboard renders for both dashboards return no widget-level
errors.

New percentile charts will populate after this change is deployed and
fresh metric samples arrive.

## Validation

- `pnpm --dir web/app exec tsc --noEmit`
- `pnpm --dir web/app exec vitest run --config ./vitest.config.mts
tests/unit/components/ClawConnectionStatus-recorder.unit.spec.tsx
tests/unit/lib/sentry/message-latency-monitor.unit.spec.ts
tests/unit/lib/sentry/connection-mismatch-monitor.unit.spec.ts`
- `pnpm --dir web/app exec eslint
tests/unit/lib/sentry/message-latency-monitor.unit.spec.ts
tests/unit/lib/sentry/connection-mismatch-monitor.unit.spec.ts
src/lib/sentry/message-latency-monitor.ts
src/lib/sentry/connection-mismatch-monitor.ts`
- `sentry dashboard view ... | jq '[.widgets[] | select(.data.type ==
"error")]'` returned `[]` for both dashboards.

files.patch:
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-# Connection-Status Mismatch Instrumentation (Sentry Logs)
+# Connection-Status Mismatch Instrumentation (Sentry Logs + Metrics)
 
 **Date**: 2026-06-12
 **Status**: Implemented
@@ -35,7 +35,7 @@ This is monitoring only. No fix, no behavior change.
 | `chat.send.outcome` | info | **none** | Denominator + full display×reality matrix. One log per send ATTEMPT (`send_ok` true/false, `failure_kind`), carrying `status_mismatch` so affected/total sends and users come from one source. |
 | `chat.connection.status_mismatch` | warn | 60s per `session:mechanism:proof` | Numerator + mechanism attribution. Emitted on send-success or bot-reply while display is degraded. |
 | `mm.connection.transition` | info (→connected) / warn (anomaly) | 60s on warn side | MM connection anomaly stats; `→connected` doubles as "opened chat today" denominator. |
-| `chat.connection.display_episode` | info | edge-triggered (6s grace smooths flapping) | EVERY degraded display, independent of proof-of-life: `phase:start` on entering reconnecting/restarting/initializing, `phase:end` (recovered/unmounted) with `episode_duration_ms`. Key ratio: mismatch events / episode starts = share of degraded displays that were provably false. |

author: shana-srp
title: fix(ios): horizontally scrollable chat tables with max-width wrapping (#2442)
message:
fix(ios): horizontally scrollable chat tables with max-width wrapping (#2442)

## What & why

Chat markdown tables rendered with each column squeezed into the bubble
width, wrapping wide cells character-by-character (e.g. `PPT / Mast /
er：`).
This makes any table with non-trivial content unreadable on iPhone.

## Approach

MarkdownUI's built-in table locks each row's height to its single-line
measurement *before* constraining column widths, so simply capping a
column
makes long cells either truncate or overflow (rows overlap). Verified
both
failure modes on-device before switching strategy.

Standard GFM tables are now intercepted and rendered by a custom view:

- **`ContentSegment.parse`** splits GFM pipe tables into a `.table`
segment
(skipping code fences) and feeds the rest through the existing inline
pass.
- **`MarkdownTableView`** sizes each column to its content up to
`tableCellMaxWidth` (220pt), then wraps onto multiple lines. Rows grow
to
the tallest cell (plain `HStack`/`VStack`, no Grid), and the whole table
  scrolls horizontally when columns overflow the bubble.
- Column widths come from Core Text measurement, with CJK font fallback.
- Keeps the `.pandaAssistant` editorial style: Lora serif, semibold
header
  row + first column, horizontal rules only, `#d8d8d8` / `#212121`.
- MarkdownUI's table style is retained as a no-wrap + horizontal-scroll
  **fallback** for any table the parser misses.
- `ContentSegment` moved to its own file to stay under the 500-line
SwiftLint
limit; removed the now-unused `LoraTableLayout` (old forced 2:1:1
widths).

## Testing

- 7 new parser tests (extraction, before/after text, alignment,
ragged-row
normalization, code-fence protection, escaped pipes, non-table prose) —
  full `ContentSegmentTests` suite (23 tests) passes.
- `xcodebuild` build succeeds (iPhone 17 Pro simulator); SwiftLint
clean.
- Rendering verified on-device via a temporary harness (removed from
this PR):
long cells wrap at the cap, rows don't overlap, table scrolls to reveal
  off-screen columns.

> Verified against a sample table mirroring the bug report, not a live
signed-in
> chat session (the component is the same `MarkdownTextView` /
`.pandaAssistant`
> path used in chat).

Co-authored-by: shiyang <shiyang@shiyangdeMacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

(skipping code fences) and feeds the rest through the existing inline
pass.
- **`MarkdownTableView`** sizes each column to its content up to
`tableCellMaxWidth` (220pt), then wraps onto multiple lines. Rows grow
to
the tallest cell (plain `HStack`/`VStack`, no Grid), and the whole table
  scrolls horizontally when columns overflow the bubble.
- Column widths come from Core Text measurement, with CJK font fallback.
- Keeps the `.pandaAssistant` editorial style: Lora serif, semibold
header
  row + first column, horizontal rules only, `#d8d8d8` / `#212121`.
- MarkdownUI's table style is retained as a no-wrap + horizontal-scroll
  **fallback** for any table the parser misses.
- `ContentSegment` moved to its own file to stay under the 500-line
SwiftLint
limit; removed the now-unused `LoraTableLayout` (old forced 2:1:1
widths).

## Testing

- 7 new parser tests (extraction, before/after text, alignment,
ragged-row
normalization, code-fence protection, escaped pipes, non-table prose) —
  full `ContentSegmentTests` suite (23 tests) passes.
- `xcodebuild` build succeeds (iPhone 17 Pro simulator); SwiftLint
clean.
- Rendering verified on-device via a temporary harness (removed from
this PR):
long cells wrap at the cap, rows don't overlap, table scrolls to reveal
  off-screen columns.

> Verified against a sample table mirroring the bug report, not a live
signed-in
> chat session (the component is the same `MarkdownTextView` /
`.pandaAssistant`
> path used in chat).

verification.payload:
tree 08fa9add93b3f183dd4e7655db53a6bd41c40ca1
parent 7c6766feb7981061831ddb80f9a7875641cc54e0
author shana-srp <shana@srp.one> 1781504274 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1781504274 +0000

fix(ios): horizontally scrollable chat tables with max-width wrapping (#2442)

## What & why

Chat markdown tables rendered with each column squeezed into the bubble
width, wrapping wide cells character-by-character (e.g. `PPT / Mast /
er：`).
This makes any table with non-trivial content unreadable on iPhone.

## Approach

MarkdownUI's built-in table locks each row's height to its single-line
measurement *before* constraining column widths, so simply capping a
column
makes long cells either truncate or overflow (rows overlap). Verified
both
failure modes on-device before switching strategy.

Standard GFM tables are now intercepted and rendered by a custom view:

- **`ContentSegment.parse`** splits GFM pipe tables into a `.table`
segment
(skipping code fences) and feeds the rest through the existing inline
pass.
- **`MarkdownTableView`** sizes each column to its content up to
`tableCellMaxWidth` (220pt), then wraps onto multiple lines. Rows grow
to
the tallest cell (plain `HStack`/`VStack`, no Grid), and the whole table
  scrolls horizontally when columns overflow the bubble.
- Column widths come from Core Text measurement, with CJK font fallback.
- Keeps the `.pandaAssistant` editorial style: Lora serif, semibold
header
  row + first column, horizontal rules only, `#d8d8d8` / `#212121`.
- MarkdownUI's table style is retained as a no-wrap + horizontal-scroll
  **fallback** for any table the parser misses.
- `ContentSegment` moved to its own file to stay under the 500-line
SwiftLint
limit; removed the now-unused `LoraTableLayout` (old forced 2:1:1
widths).

## Testing

- 7 new parser tests (extraction, before/after text, alignment,
ragged-row
normalization, code-fence protection, escaped pipes, non-table prose) —
  full `ContentSegmentTests` suite (23 tests) passes.
- `xcodebuild` build succeeds (iPhone 17 Pro simulator); SwiftLint
clean.
- Rendering verified on-device via a temporary harness (removed from
this PR):
long cells wrap at the cap, rows don't overlap, table scrolls to reveal
  off-screen columns.

> Verified against a sample table mirroring the bug report, not a live
signed-in
> chat session (the component is the same `MarkdownTextView` /
`.pandaAssistant`
> path used in chat).

Co-authored-by: shiyang <shiyang@shiyangdeMacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -249,6 +249,16 @@ enum AppTheme {

         static let inlineCodeSize: CGFloat = 13

+
+        // MARK: Table cell width (horizontal-scroll layout)

+        /// readable width instead of collapsing.
+        static let tableCellMinWidth: CGFloat = 60
+        /// Max cell width: a cell sizes to its content up to this cap, then

+        /// long cell from forcing an endlessly wide column while the table as a

         static let taskMarkerSize: CGFloat = 15
         static let footerHintSize: CGFloat = 12

@@ -0,0 +1,182 @@
+import Foundation
+
+// MARK: - Content Segment
+
+struct ContentSegment: Identifiable {
+    /// Deterministic ID based on position + type so the same content produces
+    /// the same IDs across renders (preventing unnecessary view remounts).
+    /// Includes a counter to disambiguate duplicate URLs.
+    let id: String
+    let kind: Kind
+
+    enum Kind {
+        case markdown(String)
+        case audio(URL)
+        case image(url: URL, alt: String)
+        case video(URL)
+        case table(MarkdownTable)
+    }
+
+    /// Top-level parse: first split GFM tables into `.table` segments (so they
+    /// render via our custom `MarkdownTableView` instead of MarkdownUI's table),
+    /// then run the inline pass (images/audio/video/code) on the text between
+    /// them. Table blocks are detected line-wise and skip code fences.
+    static func parse(_ content: String) -> [ContentSegment] {
+        var segments: [ContentSegment] = []
+        var counter = 0
+
+        func appendInline(_ text: String) {
+            guard !text.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty else { return }
+            for seg in parseInline(text) {
+                // Re-key with a global counter so IDs stay unique + deterministic
+                // across the multiple inline blocks a tabled message produces.
+                segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-\(seg.id)", kind: seg.kind))
+                counter += 1
+            }
+        }
+
+        let lines = content.components(separatedBy: "\n")
+        var textBuffer: [String] = []
+        var inFence = false
+        var i = 0
+        while i < lines.count {
+            let line = lines[i]
+            let trimmed = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
+
+            if trimmed.hasPrefix("```") {
+                inFence.toggle()
+                textBuffer.append(line)
+                i += 1
+                continue
+            }
+
+            // A table starts when this line and the next form header + delimiter.
+            if !inFence,
+               line.contains("|"),
+               i + 1 < lines.count,
+               MarkdownTable.isDelimiterRow(lines[i + 1]),
+               !MarkdownTable.splitCells(line).isEmpty {
+                let headerLine = line
+                let delimiterLine = lines[i + 1]
+                var bodyLines: [String] = []
+                var j = i + 2
+                while j < lines.count {
+                    let rowTrimmed = lines[j].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
+                    if rowTrimmed.isEmpty || rowTrimmed.hasPrefix("```") || !rowTrimmed.contains("|") {
+                        break
+                    }
+                    bodyLines.append(lines[j])
+                    j += 1
+                }
+                // Flush buffered text, emit the table, advance past it.
+                appendInline(textBuffer.joined(separator: "\n"))
+                textBuffer.removeAll()
+                let table = MarkdownTable.make(
+                    headerLine: headerLine,
+                    delimiterLine: delimiterLine,
+                    bodyLines: bodyLines
+                )
+                segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-table", kind: .table(table)))
+                counter += 1
+                i = j
+                continue
+            }
+
+            textBuffer.append(line)
+            i += 1
+        }
+        appendInline(textBuffer.joined(separator: "\n"))
+
+        return segments.isEmpty ? [ContentSegment(id: "0-md", kind: .markdown(content))] : segments
+    }
+
+    /// Parse content into interleaved markdown, audio, image, and video segments.
+    /// Images are extracted so they bypass MarkdownUI's built-in image rendering
+    /// (which ignores our ImageProvider and renders at full width).
+    /// Code fences are preserved as markdown so `![](url)` inside them is not extracted.
+    static func parseInline(_ content: String) -> [ContentSegment] {
+        // Step 1: split on code spans to protect their content from image extraction.
+        // Order: audio fences | generic code fences | inline code | images.
+        // Each earlier alternative consumes the match so later ones can't extract from it.
+        let audioPattern = #"```audio\n(.*?)\n```"#
+        let fencePattern = #"```[\s\S]*?```"#
+        let inlineCodePattern = #"`[^`]+`"#
+        // URL group allows one level of balanced parentheses: path(1).png
+        let imagePattern = #"!\[([^\]]*)\]\(((?:[^()\s]|\([^)]*\))+)\)"#
+
+        // Combined: audio (group 1) | fences | inline code | images (groups 2+3)
+        let combined = "(?:\(audioPattern))|(?:\(fencePattern))|(?:\(inlineCodePattern))|(?:\(imagePattern))"
+
+        guard let regex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: combined, options: .dotMatchesLineSeparators) else {
+            return [ContentSegment(id: "0-md", kind: .markdown(content))]
+        }
+        let ns = content as NSString
+        let matches = regex.matches(in: content, range: NSRange(location: 0, length: ns.length))
+
+        var segments: [ContentSegment] = []
+        var counter = 0
+        var lastEnd = content.startIndex
+
+        for match in matches {
+            guard let matchRange = Range(match.range, in: content) else { continue }
+            let matchedText = String(content[matchRange])
+
+            // Inline code: skip entirely. Only in the regex to prevent image
+            // extraction from inside backtick spans. Don't split the text —
+            // the code span stays as part of the surrounding markdown.
+            if !matchedText.hasPrefix("```"), matchedText.hasPrefix("`") {
+                continue
+            }
+
+            // Text before this match
+            let before = String(content[lastEnd..<matchRange.lowerBound])
+            if !before.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty {
+                segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-md", kind: .markdown(before)))
+                counter += 1
+            }
+
+            // Audio fence: capture group 1 (url inside ```audio\n...\n```)
+            if match.range(at: 1).location != NSNotFound,
+               let urlRange = Range(match.range(at: 1), in: content) {
+                let urlStr = String(content[urlRange]).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
+                if let url = URL(string: urlStr) {
+                    segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-audio", kind: .audio(url)))
+                    counter += 1
+                }
+            }
+            // Image: capture groups 2 (alt) and 3 (url)
+            else if match.range(at: 3).location != NSNotFound,
+                    let urlRange = Range(match.range(at: 3), in: content),
+                    let url = URL(string: String(content[urlRange])) {
+                let alt: String
+                if match.range(at: 2).location != NSNotFound,
+                   let altRange = Range(match.range(at: 2), in: content) {
+                    alt = String(content[altRange])
+                } else {
+                    alt = ""
+                }
+                if URLPreprocessor.videoExtensions.contains(url.pathExtension.lowercased()) {
+                    segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-vid", kind: .video(url)))
+                } else {
+                    segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-img", kind: .image(url: url, alt: alt)))
+                }
+                counter += 1
+            }
+            // Code fence (not inline code) — keep as markdown
+            else if matchedText.hasPrefix("```") {
+                segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-md", kind: .markdown(matchedText)))
+                counter += 1
+            }
+
+            lastEnd = matchRange.upperBound
+        }
+
+        // Remaining text
+        let tail = String(content[lastEnd...])
+        if !tail.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty {
+            segments.append(ContentSegment(id: "\(counter)-md", kind: .markdown(tail)))
+        }
+
+        return segments.isEmpty ? [ContentSegment(id: "0-md", kind: .markdown(content))] : segments
+    }
+}

@@ -220,4 +220,119 @@ struct ContentSegmentTests {
         let segments = ContentSegment.parse("")
         #expect(segments.count == 1)
     }
+
+    // MARK: - GFM tables
+
+    private func firstTable(_ segments: [ContentSegment]) -> MarkdownTable? {
+        for segment in segments {
+            if case .table(let table) = segment.kind { return table }
+        }
+        return nil
+    }
+
+    @Test("Pipe table is extracted as a .table segment")
+    func extractsTable() {
+        let content = """
+        Intro line
+
+        | # | 类型 | 角色 |
+        |---|------|------|
+        | 1 | 封面 | rhythm |
+        | 2 | 痛点 | primary |
+        """
+        let segments = ContentSegment.parse(content)
+        guard let table = firstTable(segments) else {
+            Issue.record("Expected a .table segment")
+            return
+        }
+        #expect(table.headers == ["#", "类型", "角色"])
+        #expect(table.rows.count == 2)
+        #expect(table.rows[0] == ["1", "封面", "rhythm"])
+        #expect(table.rows[1] == ["2", "痛点", "primary"])
+    }
+
+    @Test("Text before and after a table is kept as markdown")
+    func textAroundTable() {
+        let content = """
+        Before
+
+        | a | b |
+        |---|---|
+        | 1 | 2 |
+
+        After
+        """
+        let segments = ContentSegment.parse(content)
+        let markdowns = segments.compactMap { seg -> String? in
+            if case .markdown(let t) = seg.kind { return t }
+            return nil
+        }
+        #expect(firstTable(segments) != nil)
+        #expect(markdowns.contains { $0.contains("Before") })
+        #expect(markdowns.contains { $0.contains("After") })
+    }
+
+    @Test("Column alignments are parsed from the delimiter row")
+    func tableAlignments() {
+        let content = """
+        | left | center | right |
+        |:-----|:------:|------:|
+        | a | b | c |
+        """
+        guard let table = firstTable(ContentSegment.parse(content)) else {
+            Issue.record("Expected a table")
+            return
+        }
+        #expect(table.alignments == [.leading, .center, .trailing])
+    }
+
+    @Test("Ragged rows are normalized to the header column count")
+    func raggedRowsNormalized() {
+        let content = """
+        | a | b | c |
+        |---|---|---|
+        | 1 | 2 |
+        | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 |
+        """
+        guard let table = firstTable(ContentSegment.parse(content)) else {
+            Issue.record("Expected a table")
+            return
+        }
+        #expect(table.rows[0] == ["1", "2", ""])
+        #expect(table.rows[1] == ["1", "2", "3"])
+    }
+
+    @Test("A pipe inside a code fence is NOT parsed as a table")
+    func pipeInsideCodeFenceNotTable() {
+        let content = """
+        ```
+        | not | a | table |
+        |-----|---|-------|
+        | x | y | z |
+        ```
+        """
+        let segments = ContentSegment.parse(content)
+        #expect(firstTable(segments) == nil, "Tables inside code fences must not be extracted")
+    }
+
+    @Test("Escaped pipe stays inside a single cell")
+    func escapedPipeInCell() {
+        let content = """
+        | a | b |
+        |---|---|
+        | x \\| y | z |
+        """
+        guard let table = firstTable(ContentSegment.parse(content)) else {
+            Issue.record("Expected a table")
+            return
+        }
+        #expect(table.rows[0] == ["x | y", "z"])
+    }
+
+    @Test("A single pipe line without a delimiter row is not a table")
+    func singlePipeLineNotTable() {
+        let content = "a | b | c is just prose"
+        let segments = ContentSegment.parse(content)
+        #expect(firstTable(segments) == nil)
+    }
 }

author: Chris@ZooClaw
title: chore(agents): refresh Codex MCP package resolution (#2445)
message:
chore(agents): refresh Codex MCP package resolution (#2445)

## Summary

- use npm `@latest` resolution for Codex Context7 and Playwright MCP
packages
- keep npm `--min-release-age=7` so MCP startup respects the repo npm
freshness policy
- update agent skill/config drift validation for the new MCP package
policy

## Validation

- `codex --strict-config --version`
- `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh`
- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- `npm view @upstash/context7-mcp@latest version --min-release-age=7` ->
`3.2.1`
- `npm view @playwright/mcp@latest version --min-release-age=7` ->
`0.0.76`

 statusMessage = "Checking branch freshness"
 
 [mcp_servers.context7]
