-args = ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp@3.2.0"]
+command = "pnpm"
+args = ["--config.minimumReleaseAge=10080", "--config.minimumReleaseAgeStrict=true", "dlx", "--silent", "--package", "@upstash/context7-mcp@latest", "--", "context7-mcp"]
 
 [mcp_servers.playwright]
-command = "npx"
-args = ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@0.0.76"]
+command = "pnpm"
+args = ["--config.minimumReleaseAge=10080", "--config.minimumReleaseAgeStrict=true", "dlx", "--silent", "--package", "@playwright/mcp@latest", "--", "playwright-mcp"]
 
 [plugins."sentry@openai-curated"]
 enabled = true

@@ -46,7 +46,7 @@ Monorepo: Next.js frontend (`web/`) + Python FastAPI backend (`services/claw-int
 - **`.claude/commands/` files are only slash-command adapters**. Keep them as short wrappers that point to the matching `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`; do not copy runbooks or workflow logic into command files.
 - **`skills-lock.json` tracks only external vendored skill state**. External skills that the repo depends on are checked in as materialized files under `.agents/skills` and recorded in `skills-lock.json` with source/hash metadata. First-party repo skills are tracked by Git and are not hash-locked there. Do not rely on session startup to install skills.
 - **`npx skills` is only for explicit manual refreshes of vendored skills**. It is not used for Context7, Playwright, Codex plugins, Claude plugins, or normal session startup. After refreshing an external skill, check in both the updated `.agents/skills/...` files and `skills-lock.json`.
-- **Codex repo config lives in `.codex/config.toml`**. It declares Codex project hooks plus repo-level Context7/Playwright MCP servers with pinned package versions. This is separate from Claude's `.claude/settings.json`.
+- **Codex repo config lives in `.codex/config.toml`**. It declares Codex project hooks plus repo-level Context7/Playwright MCP servers. These MCP packages intentionally use `pnpm dlx` with `@latest` plus `minimumReleaseAge=10080` (7 days), so Codex sessions follow upstream MCP releases only after the same freshness buffer used by the workspace npm policy. This is separate from Claude's `.claude/settings.json`.
 - **Sentry stays enabled as a plugin skill in both tools**. Claude uses `sentry@claude-plugins-official` plus `sentry-cli@claude-plugins-official`; Codex uses `sentry@openai-curated`. Sentry MCP/app/CLI surfaces handle live data access, while the Sentry skill remains useful for SDK/API knowledge and triage workflow guidance.
 - **No repo `.mcp.json` currently**. Add one only if we need a cross-tool project MCP server with stable command, auth, environment, and failure-mode docs. Context7/Playwright are currently configured per tool (`.codex/config.toml` for Codex, `.claude/settings.json` for Claude).
 - **Local harness failures start with `bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh`**. It is read-only and distinguishes repo issues from local-only fixes such as Codex profile access to `~/.gitconfig`, Apple Command Line Tools `/usr/bin/git` failures, token-bearing remotes, `~/.npmrc` permission concerns, and missing core tools.

+
+bad_codex_config="$tmp_dir/config.toml"
+cp .codex/config.toml "$bad_codex_config"
+awk '
+  $0 == "[mcp_servers.playwright]" {
+    in_playwright = 1
+    print
+    next
+  }
+  /^\[/ {
+    in_playwright = 0
+  }
+  in_playwright && $0 == "command = \"pnpm\"" {
+    print "command = \"npx\""
+    next
+  }
+  { print }
+' "$bad_codex_config" >"$bad_codex_config.tmp"
+mv "$bad_codex_config.tmp" "$bad_codex_config"
+
+if CODEX_CONFIG_PATH="$bad_codex_config" bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --check >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  printf 'sync-agent-skills-test: MCP validation should reject per-section command drift\n' >&2
+  exit 1
+fi

author: bill-srp
title: fix(chat-replay): allow session channel ownership (#2443)
message:
fix(chat-replay): allow session channel ownership (#2443)

## Summary
- Allow chat replay creation from a user's current-org agent workspace
session channel as well as its DM channel.
- Replace the primary-computer runtime projection in replay ownership
checks with direct active Mattermost workspace lookup.
- Add regression coverage for session-channel replay creation and for
avoiding the primary-computer runtime path.

## Root cause
Conversation-thread replay passes the workspace session channel id, but
the create service only considered DM channel ids from the
primary-computer runtime projection when building the owned-channel
allowlist.

## Test plan
- [x] ruff format --check .
- [x] ruff check .
- [x] uv run --no-project --python 3.12 pytest
tests/unit/test_chat_replay_create.py -q
- [x] uv run --no-project --python 3.12 python -m py_compile
services/claw-interface/app/services/chat_replay/create.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_chat_replay_create.py
- [ ] pyright app tests (not run locally: pyright command not available
in this environment)

title: chore(hooks): support macOS session fetch fallback (#2444)
message:
chore(hooks): support macOS session fetch fallback (#2444)

Summary
- Resolve timeout command portability in the session-start fetch hook.
- Use timeout or gtimeout when available, and fall back to plain git
fetch on macOS.

Validation
- bash -n scripts/hooks/session-start-fetch.sh
- PATH=/usr/bin:/bin CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=... bash
scripts/hooks/session-start-fetch.sh
- git diff --check
- pre-push verify-changed: no locally verifiable surfaces changed

files.patch:
@@ -18,8 +18,25 @@ set -uo pipefail
 project_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
 cd "$project_dir" 2>/dev/null || exit 0
 
-# Best-effort, time-boxed so a slow/absent network can't stall session start.
-if ! timeout 20 git fetch --prune --quiet origin main 2>/dev/null; then
+fetch_origin_main() {
+  local timeout_cmd=""
+
+  if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    timeout_cmd="$(command -v timeout)"
+  elif command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    timeout_cmd="$(command -v gtimeout)"
+  fi
+
+  if [ -n "$timeout_cmd" ]; then
+    "$timeout_cmd" 20 git fetch --prune --quiet origin main
+  else
+    git fetch --prune --quiet origin main
+  fi
+}
+
+# Best-effort, time-boxed when timeout/gtimeout exists so a slow/absent network
+# can't stall session start. macOS does not ship GNU timeout by default.
+if ! fetch_origin_main 2>/dev/null; then
   echo "session-start: could not fetch origin/main (offline or no remote) — skipping freshness check."
   exit 0
 fi

author: Chris@ZooClaw
title: chore(agents): harden Codex local harness (#2437)
message:
chore(agents): harden Codex local harness (#2437)

## Summary

- add Codex-facing local harness helpers: `codex-doctor`,
`gh-body-file`, `verify-local`, and focused shell tests
- promote recurring Claude command workflows into shared
`.agents/skills` with Claude symlink projection
- refresh PR/release/Lark guidance and wire agent/harness checks into
`verify-changed`
- include the 2026-06-14 optimization plan as the evidence/rationale doc

## Verification

- `bash -n scripts/codex-doctor.sh scripts/gh-body-file.sh
scripts/verify-local.sh scripts/codex-harness.test.sh
scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- `bash scripts/codex-harness.test.sh`
- `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --check`
- `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh`
- `bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --no-network --skip-git --skip-pnpm
--skip-codex-config`
- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- pre-push hook: PR size budget and changed-surface verification passed

## Local-only follow-up

`bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh` reports one remaining local environment
failure: the current Git remote embeds credentials. That should be fixed
outside this PR by replacing the remote with clean HTTPS/SSH plus the
existing credential helper / `gh auth`.

+---
+name: codex-doctor
+description: "Diagnose local Codex, Git, pnpm, GitHub CLI, and repo harness configuration issues without mutating the repo. Use when local tooling fails before assuming code is broken."
+---
+# Codex Doctor
+
+Run the read-only local harness preflight:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh
+```
+
+Use this before retry loops when Codex reports surprising local failures around Git, `gh`, `pnpm`, npm config, repo harness scripts, or Codex project config.
+
+Important rules:
+- This script reports local-only fixes; do not commit changes to user home config from the repo PR.
+- A `~/.gitconfig` permission error means the active Codex profile or sanitized Git config path needs attention, not that repository code is broken.
+- `/usr/bin/git` with an invalid developer path is a local Apple Command Line Tools issue.
+- `~/.npmrc` permission errors should be audited before granting read access because the file may contain registry tokens.
+- Token-bearing Git remotes must be replaced with clean HTTPS/SSH plus a credential helper; never derive `GH_TOKEN` from a remote URL.
+- For srpone repos, `gh auth status` must show the expected account as active, not only logged in.
+
+Useful narrower runs:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --no-network
+bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --no-network --skip-git --skip-pnpm --skip-codex-config
+bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --strict
+```
+
+Team/local assumptions are configurable with environment variables. Set `CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER` when you want to enforce a specific srpone GitHub account, for example:
+
+```bash
+CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER=chris-srp bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh
+CODEX_DOCTOR_ZOOCLAW_ROOT="$HOME/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw" bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh
+```

+---
+name: dev-staging
+description: "Run local web/app against the real ECAP staging backend. Use for former /dev-staging."
+---
+# Dev Staging
+
+Use local Next.js with the real staging backend when mock coverage is insufficient:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/dev-staging.sh
+```
+
+Prerequisites:
+- `web/app/.env.staging.local` exists and contains staging public variables plus any needed secrets.
+- `gh auth status` is using the work account when reading GitHub environment variables.
+- `web/app/.env` and `web/app/.env.local` do not override backend-routing keys such as `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, `CLAW_INTERFACE_URL`, or `BACKEND_URL`.
+
+Use staging for real SSE/bot replies, onboarding server-side validation, and real contract shapes. Avoid write-heavy or cost-bearing shared staging actions unless the user explicitly authorizes them.
+
+After the script prints the dev URL, warm `/en` with curl before browser automation because the first Next.js compile can exceed default navigation timeouts.

+---
+name: github-safe-body
+description: "Write GitHub PR, issue, and comment bodies safely from files. Use for Markdown containing backticks, shell variables, angle brackets, or user-generated content."
+---
+# GitHub Safe Body
+
+Never pass Markdown bodies inline to `gh` when they contain backticks, shell variables, command snippets, angle brackets, or user-generated text. Write the body to a file and pass the file path.
+
+Preferred helper:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh pr-comment --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --pr <number> --body-file <file>
+bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh pr-edit --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --pr <number> --body-file <file>
+bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh issue-create --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --title "<title>" --body-file <file>
+bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh issue-edit --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --issue <number> --body-file <file>
+```
+
+Preview without sending:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh pr-comment --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --pr <number> --body-file <file> --dry-run
+```
+
+Rules:
+- Do not use `gh pr create/edit --body "...` with Markdown.
+- Do not use raw `-F body=...` or `-f body=...` for Markdown body text.
+- Do not extract credentials from `git remote get-url origin`; fix the remote or `gh auth` instead.
+- For PR creation, `gh pr create -F <file>` is acceptable because the body is file-backed.

+- If a message sends but no bot reply arrives, separate frontend delivery/SSE evidence from control-plane failures before changing frontend code.

+---
+name: verify-local
+description: "Run ECAP local verification: changed-surface checks, web/backend static checks, and optional mock or staging smoke validation. Use for former /verify-local."
+---
+# Verify Local
+
+Use the orchestrator for a single local validation entrypoint:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --changed
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --web-static web/app/src/path/to/file.tsx
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --py-static
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --mock-smoke
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --staging-smoke
+```
+
+With no mode, it defaults to `--changed`.
+
+Guidelines:
+- Prefer `--changed` before push; it delegates to `scripts/verify-changed.sh`.
+- For frontend static checks, use `scripts/verify-web.sh` through this wrapper or directly. It runs from `web/app`, clears stale `.next/types`, and uses `pnpm`.
+- For backend static checks, use `scripts/verify-py.sh`.
+- Use `--mock-smoke` for local browser-level confidence without staging credentials. It starts `scripts/dev-mock.sh`, reads the printed URL, and curls `/en`.
+- Use `--staging-smoke` only when `web/app/.env.staging.local` is prepared and a real staging login path is needed.
+- Screenshots belong under `.screenshots/`.

@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+---
+name: watch-ci
+description: "Wait for ECAP PR checks to settle and summarize failures safely. Use for former /watch-ci."
+---
+# Watch CI
+
+Use the repository watcher instead of ad hoc sleep loops:
+
+```bash
+bash scripts/watch-pr-checks.sh <pr-number>
+```
+
+Rules:
+- Do not treat `gh pr checks` exit code 8 as a failed check; it means checks are still pending.
+- Do not build `sleep N && gh pr checks` loops in the agent harness.
+- CI tests the merge ref `refs/pull/N/merge`, so a CI-only failure may be a semantic conflict with newly landed `main`.
+- For failures, inspect the failing job logs, reproduce the smallest relevant target locally, and stop after repeated unclear failures.
+
+Report the PR number, settled check state, failed check names if any, and the next concrete action.

-- **关键：确认 `web/app/.env` / `web/app/.env.local` 不会覆盖 staging 值**（`ls -la web/app/.env web/app/.env.local`）。原因见步骤 4：`next dev` 用 `override: true` 加载 cwd(`web/app/`) 下这两个文件，它们设的 routing 键（`NEXT_PUBLIC_*` / `CLAW_INTERFACE_URL` / `BACKEND_URL` 等）会覆盖 dotenv 注入的 staging 值，静默打回本地/prod。

-  - `scripts/dev-staging.sh` 已内置这个判断：只在检测到真实 routing 键冲突时拦下，否则放行（不会因为符号链接存在就报错）。

-### 步骤 2：拉取 staging 公开配置
-- 运行：

-  ```
-- 这就是 staging 部署实际注入的公开变量全集（Firebase 七件套、`NEXT_PUBLIC_ACCOUNT_URL`、`NEXT_PUBLIC_MATTERMOST_URL`、`CLAW_INTERFACE_URL`/`BACKEND_URL`、`LITELLM_PROXY_URL`、`R2_ENDPOINT_URL` + ID、`NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN`、`NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` 等）。

-## 真实登录态（mock 没有的那一步）
-
-连真实 staging 验证不需要 CF token（staging 无 Access app），唯一的硬门槛是**可复现的真实登录态**。mock 用 localStorage 注入假 token 绕过；真实 staging 不吃假 token，必须有真会话。
-
-- **先确认一个后端依赖（否则三种登录都白搭）**：staging account 服务必须放行 `http://localhost:<dev端口>` 的 CORS origin，否则 OTP/登录直接被 CORS 拦（本地改不了，需后端配；见下方 gotcha）。先确认这个再往下投入。
-- **拿到真实会话的三条路（按推荐度）**：
-  1. **capture-and-reuse（推荐）**：在真机/已登录的 staging 会话里，把真实 `gensmo:auth:user_info`（含真 `access_token`）拷到本地 DevTools；或用 Playwright `storageState`（对标 e2e 的 `tests/e2e/.auth/user.json`）存一次复用，到期再重捕。文档化 token 过期时长 + 重捕步骤。
-  2. **每次手动 OTP 登录**：最简单，但每次有人工 OTP 摩擦。
-  3. **专用 staging 测试账号 + 可编程读 OTP**（测试邮箱/固定 OTP）：最顺滑，但要 infra/后端建账号配合。
-- **覆盖边界**：真路径独有覆盖 = 真实 SSE/bot 回复、onboarding 服务端校验、真实契约形状 / Mongo 数据（正是 mock 盲区）。仍要小心**写**操作会污染共享 staging（真实 Stripe 扣费、真实 LLM 花费）——**以只读验证为主**。
-
-## 已知 gotcha（主动告知用户，免得重复排查）
-
-- **`/tips/*` 在 staging 必 404，不是 bug**：tips 由兄弟仓库 `zooclaw-tips` 的独立 Worker 服务，只挂生产 zone `zooclaw.ai`；staging 域名无此路由，i18n 中间件会把 `/tips/en/` 变 `/en/tips/en` 然后 404。
-- **account 服务 CORS**：`NEXT_PUBLIC_ACCOUNT_URL` 浏览器直连，staging account 服务需放行 `http://localhost:<dev端口>` origin，否则 OTP/登录被 CORS 拦（需后端配，本地改不了）。**注意 dev 端口会漂**（3000 被占 → 3002/3006…），放行名单必须覆盖实际端口，否则只放 `:3000` 在漂移后失效。
-- **CF Access 边界**：staging `claw-interface` 没有任何 Cloudflare Access app 覆盖 —— BFF `/api/*` 转发不需要 CF token，从**任何**网络都能到达（不限办公室/VPN）。CF Access 只用于 production 的 `/admin`·`/internal` path，与本地前端验证无关。
-- **Firebase 必须是 staging 项目**（`srp-ecap-staging`）：用错项目 Google 登录会成功但 token 被 staging 后端拒。
-
-## 验证
-
-- 浏览器 DevTools → Network 搜 `zooclaw.ai` 应为 **0 条**（无 prod 域名泄漏）；Mattermost 请求 host 应是 `mattermost.internal.yesy.live`，账户请求是 `account.favie.yesy.live`。
-- Console 无红色报错即接入成功。
-
-> 相关：`web/app/CLAUDE.md`（前端约定）；staging 公开配置的单一事实源是 GitHub Environment `staging` 的 Variables（见步骤 2）。

+# pr
 
-## 描述
+This Claude slash command is only an adapter.
 
-这个命令会自动执行完整的 PR 创建流程，包括代码提交、lint 修复、PR 创建，以及 CI 状态监控和自动修复。
+Invoke the shared `pr` skill now:
 
-## 使用方法
+- Canonical instructions: `.agents/skills/pr/SKILL.md`
+- Claude projection: `.claude/skills/pr`
 
-```bash
-pr
-```
-
-## 执行流程
-
-### 步骤 1: 端到端自我测试
-
-在提交代码前，先对本次改动进行端到端的自我测试验证：
-
-- 使用 bash 命令、浏览器（Playwright MCP）或其他可用工具，对本次改动涉及的功能进行端到端测试
-- 验证核心功能路径（golden path）和关键边界情况
-- 检查是否引入了对现有功能的回归
-
-**跳过条件**：如果在前序开发流程中（如 `/feature-dev`、手动测试等）已经完成了端到端测试验证，则跳过此步骤，输出：`⏭️ 端到端测试已在前序流程中完成，跳过。`
-
-**失败处理**：如果测试发现问题，**停止流程**，输出问题详情，要求修复后重新运行 `/pr`。
-
-### 步骤 2: 精简代码
-
-运行 `/simplify` 技能对本次改动的代码进行精简和优化：
-
-- 审查改动的代码，检查是否有可复用、质量或效率方面的问题
-- 自动修复发现的问题（如冗余代码、不必要的抽象、命名改进等）
-- 确保精简后的代码功能不变
-
-**注意**：此步骤仅针对本次改动的文件，不会修改未变更的代码。
-
-### 步骤 3: 暂存改动
-- 执行 `git add .` 暂存所有当前改动
-- 包括新文件、修改的文件和删除的文件
-
-### 步骤 4: 检查是否有改动
-- 检查工作区是否有待提交的改动
-- 如果没有改动，命令会提前退出
-
-### 步骤 5: 提交改动
-- 自动生成简洁的 commit 消息，遵循以下规则：
-  - 使用 conventional commit 格式：`<type>: <description>`
-  - type: feat/fix/refactor/chore/docs 等
-  - description: 简短描述改动内容，不超过 50 字符
-  - 不要添加 Co-Authored-By 或其他附加信息
-  - 示例: `feat: add virtual key auth`, `fix: resolve login bug`
-- 如果自动生成失败，使用默认消息 `chore: update files`
-
-### 步骤 6: Pre-commit Hook 自动修复
-- 如果 commit 因 pre-commit hook 失败（hook 自动修复了代码），重新暂存修复后的代码
-- 执行 `git add .` 暂存 pre-commit hook 自动修复的内容
-
-### 步骤 7: 重新提交
-- 使用相同的 commit 消息再次提交
-- 这次提交应该通过所有 pre-commit 检查
-
-### 步骤 8: 本地执行 build-and-test
-
-在推送之前，本地运行与 CI `build-and-test` 等价的检查：
-
-```bash
-pytest --cov=app --cov-report=term-missing -q
-```
-
-判断逻辑：
-- 如果测试通过且覆盖率 ≥ 90%：继续执行步骤 9
-- 如果测试失败或覆盖率不足：**停止流程**，输出失败原因，要求人工修复后重新运行 `/pr`
-  - 不要自动修复测试失败，因为测试失败通常需要人工判断
-  - 输出格式：`❌ 本地测试失败，请修复后重新运行 /pr\n\n失败详情:\n<pytest output>`
-
-### 步骤 9: 推送并创建 PR
-- 推送当前分支到远程仓库
-- 使用 GitHub CLI 创建 PR 到 main 分支
-- PR 标题和描述会自动从 commits 生成
-- 记录创建的 PR URL 和编号
-
-### 步骤 10: 监控 CI 状态（最多重试 3 次）
-
-创建 PR 后，进入 CI 监控循环（fix_attempt 从 0 开始）：
-
-#### 10a: 等待 CI 检查完成
-- 使用 `gh pr checks <PR号> --watch` 等待所有 checks 完成
-- 或每隔 30 秒轮询一次 `gh pr checks <PR号>`，直到 `auto-review` 和 `build-and-test` 都不再处于 pending/in_progress 状态
-- 最多等待 10 分钟
-
-#### 10b: 检查必须通过的两个 CI 步骤
-检查以下两个 CI check 的状态：
-- `auto-review`
-- `build-and-test`
-
-判断逻辑：
-- 如果两个 check 都是 `success` / `passing`：**CI 通过，流程完成** ✅
-- 如果任意一个是 `failure` / `failing`：**CI 失败，进入修复流程**
-- 如果 fix_attempt >= 3：**停止，通知人工处理** 🛑
-
-#### 10c: 分析并修复 CI 失败（fix_attempt < 3 时）
-
-fix_attempt 加 1，然后：
-
-**对于 `build-and-test` 失败：**
-1. 获取失败 check 的详细日志：`gh run view <run_id> --log-failed`
-2. 仔细分析错误信息（编译错误、测试失败、import 错误等）
-3. 根据错误信息定位并修复对应的源代码文件
-4. 运行本地测试验证修复（如果可行）：`python -m pytest` 或相关命令
-
-**对于 `auto-review` 失败：**
-1. 获取 auto-review 的评论：`gh pr view <PR号> --comments`
-2. 分析 review 指出的代码问题
-3. 根据 review 意见修改代码
-
-**修复后：**
-- `git add .`
-- 生成描述修复内容的 commit 消息，例如：`fix: resolve CI build error - <简短描述>`
-- `git commit -m "<fix commit message>"`
-- 如果 commit 因 pre-commit hook 失败，自动 `git add .` 后重新 commit
-- `git push`
-- 回到步骤 10a，继续监控
-
-#### 10d: 超过重试次数时
-如果已经尝试修复 3 次仍未通过，停止自动修复并输出：
-
-```
-⚠️  CI checks 在 3 次修复尝试后仍未通过，需要人工介入。
-
-PR 链接: <PR_URL>
-
-失败的 checks:

-
-最后一次错误信息:

-```

-## 前置要求
-
-- ✅ 当前目录必须是一个 Git 仓库
-- ✅ 必须安装并认证 GitHub CLI (`gh`)
-- ✅ 当前分支不能是 main 分支（建议在 feature 分支上运行）

-## 注意事项

-1. **分支要求**: 确保在 feature 分支上运行此命令，不要在 main 分支上执行
-2. **Pre-commit Hook**: 如果第一次 commit 失败，说明 hook 自动修复了代码，会自动重新 add 并再次 commit

-4. **PR 自动填充**: PR 的标题和描述会根据 commits 自动生成
-5. **CI 监控**: PR 创建后会持续监控 `auto-review` 和 `build-and-test` 两个关键 CI 检查
-6. **最多修复 3 次**: 自动修复最多尝试 3 次，超过后需要人工介入
-7. **修复提交**: 每次修复会产生独立的 fix commit，保持提交历史清晰
-

-## 常见问题
-
-**Q: 如果我想自定义 commit 消息怎么办？**
-A: 这个命令会自动生成 commit 消息。如果需要自定义，建议手动执行 `git commit` 后再使用 `gh pr create`。
-
-**Q: 可以创建 PR 到其他分支吗？**
-A: 当前固定为 main 分支。如需修改，可以编辑此命令文件中的 `--base main` 参数。
-
-**Q: 如果 GitHub CLI 没有认证怎么办？**
-A: 首先运行 `gh auth login` 完成认证。
-
-**Q: Pre-commit hook 失败会中断流程吗？**
-A: 不会。如果 hook 可以自动修复问题，会自动重新 add 和 commit。如果有无法自动修复的错误，需要手动修复后再运行命令。
-
-**Q: auto-review 和 build-and-test 以外的 CI check 失败怎么办？**
-A: 本命令只关注这两个必须通过的 check，其他 check 的失败不会触发自动修复流程。

-## Context
+# release-notes
 
-You are writing release communication for the ECAP bot platform. The audience is **regular users** (not developers) who will see:
-1. A **notification message** — short banner text shown in the chat/settings page upgrade prompt
-2. **Release notes** — expandable detail section when user clicks "View details"
+This Claude slash command is only an adapter.
 
-## Input
+Invoke the shared `release-notes` skill now:
 
-The user provides raw technical changes: `$1`
+- Canonical instructions: `.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md`
+- Claude projection: `.claude/skills/release-notes`
+- Technical changes or changelog: `$1`
 
-If no input was provided, ask the user to paste the technical changelog or describe the changes.
-
-## Rules
-
-### Notification message
-- Language: **English**
-- One sentence, under 120 characters ideally (max 180)
-- Lead with an emoji (🚀 for features, 🔧 for fixes, ⚡ for performance)
-- Highlight the 1–2 most impactful changes from the **user's perspective**
-- End with a call to action: "Upgrade now for..." or "Upgrade for..."
-- Do NOT mention internal tools, infra, or config names (no terragrunt, kustomize, k8s, etc.)
-
-### Release notes
-- Language: **English**
-- Markdown format, starts with `## What's new`
-- Each item: emoji + **bold title** + one-sentence explanation
-- Order by **user impact** (most noticeable first), not by technical order
-- Max 5 items — collapse minor/internal changes into one "reliability" bullet
-- Last line: reassurance about upgrade safety, e.g. "The upgrade takes about 1–2 minutes. Your chat history and data are preserved."
-- If a change preserves something users might worry about losing, call it out explicitly
-
-### Translation principles
-- **Hide implementation details**: users don't care about variable names, file paths, config formats
-- **Emphasize benefits, not mechanisms**: "skills persist after restart" not "JuiceFS mount added to ~/.agents/skills/"
-- **Quantify when possible**: "cost reduced by 50x" is better than "cost optimized"
-- **Internal-only changes** → "reliability/infrastructure improvements" (one generic bullet)
-- **Backward compatibility** → "no changes needed on your end" or "existing setup continues to work"
-
-## Output format
-
-Output exactly this structure (with a blank line between sections):
-
-```
-**Notification message:**
-
-> [emoji] [message text]
-
----
-
-**Release notes:**
-
-[markdown block]
-```
-
-Then add a mapping table for the user to verify:
-
-```
-| Technical change | User-facing translation |
-|---|---|
-| ... | ... |
-```
-
-## Examples
-
-### Input
-"Heartbeat 成本优化 — lightContext/isolatedSession/haiku-4.5，预估 $0.50-2.00 → <$0.01"
-
-### Output
-
-**Notification message:**
-> ⚡ Background health checks are now 50x more efficient. Upgrade to reduce idle credit consumption.
-
-**Release notes:**
-```markdown
-## What's new
-
-- ⚡ **Lower background cost** — Your bot's health checks now use a lightweight model, dramatically reducing idle credit usage (estimated 50x reduction).
-
-The upgrade takes about 1–2 minutes. Your chat history and data are preserved.
-```
-
-| Technical change | User-facing translation |
-|---|---|
-| lightContext/isolatedSession/haiku-4.5, $0.50–2.00 → <$0.01 | "health checks 50x more efficient, lower idle credit usage" |

-# Release（ecap-workspace 发布）
+---
+description: Cut an ECAP release tag through the shared repo skill
+argument-hint: <patch|minor|major>
+---
 
-把已合并到 main 的改动发布到 staging / production。本仓库**不是**手搓 `vX.Y.Z-release` tag 的流程——release tag 由 CI 在 `claude/**` PR 合并时自动创建，production 部署还卡在 GitHub Environment 人工审批门后面。这个命令帮你查清现状、推进部署、过审批门。
+# release
 
-## 背景（必读，决定为什么这么做）
+This Claude slash command is only an adapter.
 
-- **三个独立的 tag 命名空间**，各自触发各自的部署 workflow，互不相干：
-  | namespace | 触发 workflow | beta/staging | release/production |
-  |---|---|---|---|
-  | `ecap-v*`（前端 web） | `deploy.yml` | `ecap-v*-beta` | `ecap-v*-release` |
-  | `service-v*`（后端 claw-interface） | `service-deploy.yml` | `service-v*-beta` | `service-v*-release` |
-  | `ios-v*`（iOS） | `ios-deploy.yml` | `ios-v*-beta.N` | `ios-v*-rc.N`（注意是 **-rc**，不是 -release） |
-- **staging 自动**：push 到 main 即触发 `deploy.yml` 部署 staging（无 tag、无审批）。日常合并就上 staging 了。
-- **production 发布 = 手动打 `*-release` tag（常规做法）；`claude/**` PR 的自动打 tag 是少见的例外。** 手动：推一个 `ecap-v*-release` / `service-v*-release` tag 即触发对应 production 部署（见步骤 3）。自动：**仅当**合并的 PR head 分支以 `claude/` 开头时，`auto-tag-*` 的 `create-release-tag` job（`merged==true && head.ref 以 'claude/' 开头`）才自动 cut `*-release`（最新 `*-release` 基础上 PATCH+1）——日常人工分支用不到。staging 的 `*-beta.N` 同理仅由 `claude/**` 分支经 `workflow_run` 自动产生。
-  - ⚠️ **普通人工分支的 PR（head 不是 `claude/…`）合并到 main 不会自动产生任何 tag**——production 走步骤 3 的手动打 tag；staging 则在 push main 时已自动部署（无需 tag）。
-  - 注：两个 `auto-tag-*` 的 `workflow_run` 都监听共享的 "Code Quality Check"，所以每个 job 用 `dorny/paths-filter` 按改动面 gate——**前端 tag 只在 `web/app/**`（+ web/app 构建实际依赖的 `web/` 根文件：`tsconfig.base.json`、`package.json`、`pnpm-lock.yaml`、`pnpm-workspace.yaml`、`.npmrc`）变更时产生，service tag 只在 `services/claw-interface/**` 变更时产生**。因此一个只改前端的 `claude/` PR 不会再误发 `service-v*`（避免多余的后端 production 部署），反之亦然；beta 同理。（注意前端 gate 是 `web/app/**` 而非 `web/**`——后者会把 enterprise-admin/enterprise-app/dashboard-console 这些**各自独立部署**的 app 也算进来。auto-tag-service 之前还把 workflow 名误写成不存在的 "Python Code Quality"，beta 完全不触发，已修为 "Code Quality Check"。）
-- **production 卡审批门**：`deploy.yml` / `service-deploy.yml` 的 production job 设了 `environment: production`，仓库 Settings → Environments 配了 reviewer。release tag 推上去后，部署会**暂停等人工 approve**。审批人名单不在 workflow 文件里，要查 API。
+Invoke the shared `release` skill now:
 
-## 执行流程
+- Canonical instructions: `.agents/skills/release/SKILL.md`
+- Claude projection: `.claude/skills/release`
+- Requested release type, if provided: `$1`
 
-### 步骤 0：账号
-- 创建 PR / 打 tag / 审批前，确认 `gh` 当前 active 账号是你的 **SerendipityOne 工作账号**（srpone 仓库要求），不是个人账号。
-- 校验：工作账号的主邮箱以 `@srp.one` 结尾——
-  ```bash
-  gh api user --jq '.login + "  " + (.email // "<email 未公开>")'
-  ```
-  返回的邮箱是 `…@srp.one` 即为工作账号。若邮箱未公开（显示 `<email 未公开>`），改用组织成员校验：`gh api orgs/SerendipityOneInc/members/$(gh api user --jq .login)`（HTTP 204 = 是成员）。
-- 不是工作账号则用 `gh auth switch --user <你的工作账号>` 切换（`gh auth status` 列出已登录账号）。
-
-### 步骤 1：判断要发哪条 surface
-- 跨 surface 改动常常前后端都要发：看本次合并的改动落在 `web/**` 还是 `services/claw-interface/**` 还是两者。前端 only → 只 `ecap-v*`；后端 only → 只 `service-v*`；两者都改 → 两条都要。
-
-### 步骤 2：查当前各环境部署的版本
-```bash
-bash scripts/prod-version.sh
-```
-- 报告 staging / production 上前端 `/api/version` 的实际 build-info（commit / version / deployedBy）+ 最新 `ecap-v*` / `service-v*` tag + 最近 deploy run。runtime `/api/version` 比 git tag 更可信（反映真实部署的 bundle，含手动 `workflow_dispatch`）。
-
-### 步骤 3：触发 release
-- **常规路径：手动打 release tag。** 算出下一个版本（基于最新 `*-release` 递增，一般 PATCH+1），打 tag 并推送，tag 即触发对应 production 部署（卡审批门，见步骤 4）：
-  ```bash
-  git fetch --tags --quiet
-  # 前端：
-  LATEST=$(git tag -l 'ecap-v*-release' | sort -V | tail -1); echo "latest: $LATEST"   # 例 ecap-v0.9.0-release
-  git tag ecap-v<X.Y.Z>-release && git push origin ecap-v<X.Y.Z>-release
-  # 后端同理，用 service-v<X.Y.Z>-release。
-  ```
-  **tag 前缀必须精确匹配** `^ecap-v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-(beta|release)$`（后端 `service-v*`）—— 写成 `vX.Y.Z-release` 等其他前缀 `deploy.yml` 一概不触发。`-release` → production，`-beta` → staging。
-- **`claude/**` PR 自动（较少见）**：只有当合并的 PR 的 head 分支以 `claude/` 开头时，`auto-tag-*` 才会自动 cut release tag（这种 Claude 辅助开发流不常用）。若走了这条，用 `gh run list --workflow=auto-tag-frontend.yaml --limit 3` 确认 tag 已自动产生，无需手动打。
-- **workflow_dispatch 兜底**（不新建 tag、重发已部署版本）：
-  ```bash
-  gh workflow run deploy.yml -f environment=production                                  # 前端：只要 environment
-  gh workflow run service-deploy.yml -f environment=production -f version=service-v<X.Y.Z>-release  # 后端：version 必填，漏了报错
-  ```
-
-### 步骤 4：production 审批门 —— 由人工在 GitHub 网页批，agent 不要自动批
-> 🛑 **硬规则：agent 绝不调用 `pending_deployments` 的 approve API 自动放行 production。** 误批会直接造成线上事故，审批必须是人主动在 GitHub 网页上做的动作。
-- agent 只做**只读**准备，把信息交给用户：
-  ```bash
-  gh run list --workflow=deploy.yml --limit 5                                              # 找等待中的 run
-  gh api repos/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/<RUN_ID>/pending_deployments  # 确认确实卡在审批
-  gh api repos/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/environments/production                    # 看谁能批
-  ```
-- 然后**停下来**，把 run 的网页链接给用户，请用户去 GitHub Actions 页面（该 run → "Review deployments" → 勾 production → Approve）手动批准。可选：若用户要求"到审批点通知我"，用 PushNotification（ToolSearch 动态加载）在检测到 `pending_deployments` 时提醒——**只通知，不代批**。
-
-### 步骤 5：盯部署到完成
-- `gh run watch <RUN_ID>`（或后台 watcher）。完成后再跑一次 `scripts/prod-version.sh` 确认 production 的 `/api/version` commit 已是目标 commit。
-
-## 已知 gotcha
-- iOS 用 `-rc.N` 不是 `-release`；`ios-deploy.yml` 还有 `paths: ios/**` 过滤，不碰 ios 不会触发。
-- 后端**有** `/version` 端点（`services/claw-interface/app/routes/status.py`，返回 `{success, data: build_info}`，schema 与前端 `/api/version` 对齐），可核对已部署后端 commit；但 claw-interface 不像前端那样有公开 origin（在 BFF / CF Access 之后），所以 `prod-version.sh` 对后端默认靠 git tag + `gh run list --workflow=service-deploy.yml` 判断，需精确 commit 时经鉴权路径打 `/version`。后端经 Docker 镜像 + kustomize 部署到 GKE `ecap` 命名空间（见根 `CLAUDE.md` → Runtime Clusters），不是 Cloudflare。
-- staging 部署无审批门，是设计如此；只有 production 卡审批。
-

 ## Frontend toolchain invariant
 - **This is a pnpm workspace — always use `pnpm` / `pnpm exec`, never `npx`.** `npx tsc` is the classic trap: when TypeScript isn't resolvable on the local PATH (e.g. run from the repo root, which has no `node_modules`), `npx` silently fetches the unrelated npm package literally named `tsc` from the registry — a decoy that just prints "This is not the tsc command you are looking for" — instead of erroring. `pnpm exec tsc` always resolves the workspace's installed TypeScript and never touches the registry. (More broadly, `npx <x>` can run arbitrary registry packages and bypasses the pnpm-pinned toolchain; reserve it only for a tool genuinely not in the workspace.)

+# Codex Harness / Local Environment Optimization Plan
+
+> Evidence basis: local Codex session JSONL under `~/.codex/sessions` with file mtime since 2026-05-15, current memory registry, current `ecap-workspace` files, and live local checks on 2026-06-14.
+>
+> Implementation note: this report was written from the main checkout before a fresh implementation worktree was created. The fresh worktree includes the already-merged repo skill sync/Codex config baseline, so the implementation extends that existing model instead of adding it from scratch.
+
+## Executive Summary
+
+The last-30-day Codex friction is not mainly missing product code context. It is concentrated in the local harness layer:
+
+- Codex frequently needs escalation for routine development work: 3,101 escalated `exec_command` calls across 56 of 71 user-facing sessions.
+- Git and package-manager setup are still noisy under sandbox: `git status` can fail because `~/.gitconfig` is unreadable, `/usr/bin/git` is broken due missing Command Line Tools `xcrun`, and `pnpm` warns on unreadable `~/.npmrc`.
+- GitHub auth hygiene is poor: the current `origin` remote still embeds a token, and sessions repeatedly used `GH_TOKEN=$(git remote get-url origin ...)` style workarounds.
+- Repo scripts have improved a lot (`verify-web.sh`, `verify-py.sh`, `dev-mock.sh`, `dev-staging.sh`, `watch-pr-checks.sh`, CDP helpers), but Codex-facing shared skills have not caught up. `.agents/skills/pr/SKILL.md` still tells agents to run older raw `pnpm --dir web ...` and `gh pr checks` commands.
+- Claude-only commands exist for `/verify-local`, `/watch-ci`, `/dev-staging`, and `/prod-validate`; Codex does not currently get equivalent repo-owned skills from `.agents/skills`.
+- At report time, the inspected checkout had `skills-lock.json`, `.agents/skills`, and `.claude/skills`, but no `scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh` and no repo `.codex/` config directory. The implementation branch is based on newer `origin/main`, where those pieces already exist.
+
+## Evidence Snapshot
+
+Parsed session window:
+
+| Metric | Count |
+|---|---:|
+| Session files | 146 |
+| User-facing sessions | 71 |
+| Guardian / approval subagent sessions | 75 |
+| Function calls in user-facing sessions | 13,929 |
+| Escalated exec calls | 3,101 |
+| Sessions with escalated exec calls | 56 |
+| Non-zero tool outputs | 1,464 |
+
+Exact error signatures found in user-facing sessions:
+
+| Category | Output hits | Session hits | Representative failure |
+|---|---:|---:|---|
+| Sandbox filesystem / git metadata locks | 560 | 48 | `git add` failed creating `.git/index.lock`; worktree creation failed creating `.git/refs/...lock` |
+| Sandbox / network / approval friction | 204 | 43 | GitHub API and other network reads were retried outside sandbox |
+| Local bind denied | 24 | 13 | `next build` / local services hit `listen EPERM 127.0.0.1` |
+| Missing path / wrong cwd | 178 | 41 | `rg: web/src: No such file or directory`; `sed: ... No such file` |
+| Missing JS/Python modules | 49 | 17 | `Cannot find module 'typescript'`; `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml'` |
+| Broken Apple CLT path | 48 | 8 | `/usr/bin/git` failed with `xcrun: error: invalid active developer path` |
+| GitHub CLI / API shape issues | 60 | 29 | REST review-comment update 404, `gh pr checks` pending exit semantics, wrong repo context |
+| Shell quoting / Markdown body corruption | 21 | 9 | Backticks in `gh pr comment --body "..."` executed as shell commands |
+| Toolchain wrong cwd | 12 | 4 | ESLint plugin not found from `web/` instead of `web/app` |
+| Lark auth / bot membership / keychain | 63 | 31 | missing `im:message.send_as_user`, app pending approval, bot not in target chat |
+
+Repeated workarounds:
+
+| Pattern | Calls | Sessions | Why it matters |
+|---|---:|---:|---|
+| `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null` / `HOME=/private/tmp` Git workarounds | 809 | 9 | Codex lacks readable Git user config and keeps bypassing it locally. |
+| `/opt/homebrew/bin/git` explicit calls | 844 | 7 | Agents route around broken `/usr/bin/git` / CLT and PATH uncertainty. |
+| Extracting `GH_TOKEN` from `git remote get-url origin` | 25 | 3 | Token-bearing remotes leak into logs and encourage unsafe auth workarounds. |
+| Inline GitHub markdown body commands | 24 | 11 | Shell quoting repeatedly corrupts PR/issue/comment bodies. |
+| Temporary body files | 126 | 19 | This is the correct workaround and should be standardized. |
+| Hand-rolled Python/Node scan scripts | 127 | 19 | Many ad hoc repository analyzers should become reusable harness scripts or skills. |
+| `scripts/worktree.sh` | 31 | 5 | Worktree helper is valuable and should remain the standard. |
+| `scripts/verify-web.sh` / `verify-py.sh` / `dev-mock.sh` in historical Codex logs | 0 | 0 | These landed late and are not yet embedded into Codex skills, so Codex did not naturally choose them. |
+
+Current live checks:
+
+- `git status --short` failed inside the current sandbox with `fatal: unable to access '/Users/xuwenhao/.gitconfig': Operation not permitted`.
+- The active Codex profile is `default_permissions = "ecap-local-browser"` and has read access to `~/.config/git`, but not `~/.gitconfig`.
+- `/usr/bin/git --version` fails with missing `xcrun`; `xcode-select -p` points at `/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools`.
+- `/opt/homebrew/bin/git --version` also failed in sandbox because it tried to read `~/.gitconfig`.
+- `pnpm --version` works but warns: `EPERM: operation not permitted, open '/Users/xuwenhao/.npmrc'`.
+- The current Git remote embeds an access token in the URL.
+- At report time, `scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh` was absent in the inspected checkout. In the implementation worktree it is present from newer `origin/main`.
+
+## What Is Already Fixed In The Repo
+
+Do not duplicate these:
+
+- `scripts/verify-web.sh`: correct `web/app` cwd, `pnpm exec`, stale `.next/types/validator.ts` cleanup, governance guards, scoped vitest/eslint.
+- `scripts/verify-py.sh`: correct `services/claw-interface` cwd, venv-first tool resolution, fast and full backend gates.
+- `scripts/dev-mock.sh`: mock backend + Next dev, actual port printing, cold compile warning.
+- `web/app/scripts/mock-backend.mjs`: now includes SSE chat, `/session/chat/subscribe`, public replay fixtures, and `MOCK_USER_STATE=onboarding_required`.

+## AGENTS.md Updates To Make
+
+Add a Codex-specific section under "Local hooks & the push gate" or after "Frontend toolchain invariant":

+- Codex local config is primarily `~/.codex/config.toml`; repo instructions come from `AGENTS.md` and shared repo skills, not from a repo-local plugin layer.
+- If `git status` fails with `~/.gitconfig: Operation not permitted`, run `scripts/codex-doctor.sh` and fix the profile; do not default to `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null` for normal development.
+- If `/usr/bin/git` fails with `xcrun: invalid active developer path`, fix local Command Line Tools or use the documented Homebrew Git path temporarily.
+- `pnpm` warning on `~/.npmrc` means the sandbox cannot read user npm config. Do not grant access blindly if it contains tokens; use a sanitized userconfig or ask.
+- Git remotes must not include tokens. Use clean HTTPS/SSH remote plus credential helper / `gh auth`.
+- Never extract `GH_TOKEN` from `git remote get-url`.
+- For GitHub body/comment/issue text with Markdown, write a file and pass `-F body=@file` or equivalent. Do not inline shell strings with backticks.

+- `/usr/bin/git --version` or the documented canonical Git works without `xcrun`.
+- Current Zooclaw remotes no longer embed tokens.
+- `pnpm --version` no longer emits unreadable `.npmrc` warnings, or the warning is intentionally suppressed via sanitized config.
+- `.agents/skills/pr/SKILL.md` uses current repo harness scripts.
+- Shared skills exist for `verify-local`, `watch-ci`, `dev-staging`, `prod-validate`, `github-safe-body`, and `codex-doctor`.

+- A fresh Codex session can discover the new shared skills and choose them without relying on memory from this analysis.

@@ -0,0 +1,283 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# codex-doctor.sh — local preflight for recurring Codex harness failures.
+# Read-only by design: reports actionable environment/config problems, but does
+# not edit git remotes, user config, Codex profiles, or credentials.
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+CODEX_CONFIG="${CODEX_CONFIG:-$HOME/.codex/config.toml}"
+SKIP_GIT=0
+SKIP_PNPM=0
+SKIP_NETWORK=0
+SKIP_CODEX_CONFIG=0
+STRICT=0
+PRINT_ACTIVE_GH_USER_FILE=""
+EXPECTED_GH_USER="${CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER:-}"
+EXPECTED_ZOOCLAW_ROOT="${CODEX_DOCTOR_ZOOCLAW_ROOT:-$HOME/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw}"
+EXPECTED_CODEX_SESSIONS="${CODEX_DOCTOR_CODEX_SESSIONS:-$HOME/.codex/sessions}"
+EXPECTED_CODEX_PLUGIN_CACHE="${CODEX_DOCTOR_CODEX_PLUGIN_CACHE:-$HOME/.codex/plugins/cache}"
+
+# shellcheck source=scripts/lib/log.sh
+source "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/lib/log.sh"
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<EOF
+Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS]
+
+Read-only Codex/local-dev preflight. It checks Git, token-bearing remotes,
+pnpm/npmrc warnings, required repo harness scripts, core tools, and Codex config
+shape. It prints fixes for local-only items that should not go into a PR.
+
+Options:
+  --repo-root DIR          Repo root to inspect (default: current repo)
+  --codex-config FILE      Codex config to inspect (default: ~/.codex/config.toml)
+  --no-network            Skip network/auth checks such as gh auth
+  --skip-git              Skip git status/remotes checks
+  --skip-pnpm             Skip pnpm/npmrc checks
+  --skip-codex-config     Skip Codex config inspection
+  --strict                Treat warnings as failures
+  -h, --help              Show this help
+
+Environment:
+  CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER              Optional expected GitHub account for srpone repos (for example: chris-srp)
+  CODEX_DOCTOR_ZOOCLAW_ROOT         Expected Zooclaw workspace root (default: ~/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw)
+  CODEX_DOCTOR_CODEX_SESSIONS       Expected Codex sessions path (default: ~/.codex/sessions)
+  CODEX_DOCTOR_CODEX_PLUGIN_CACHE   Expected Codex plugin cache path (default: ~/.codex/plugins/cache)
+EOF
+}
+
+while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --repo-root) REPO_ROOT="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --codex-config) CODEX_CONFIG="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --no-network) SKIP_NETWORK=1; shift ;;
+    --skip-git) SKIP_GIT=1; shift ;;
+    --skip-pnpm) SKIP_PNPM=1; shift ;;
+    --skip-codex-config) SKIP_CODEX_CONFIG=1; shift ;;
+    --strict) STRICT=1; shift ;;
+    --print-active-gh-user-from-file) PRINT_ACTIVE_GH_USER_FILE="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+    *) err "Unknown option: $1"; usage >&2; exit 2 ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+extract_active_gh_user() {
+  awk '
+    /Logged in to github.com account / {
+      for (i = 1; i <= NF; i++) {
+        if ($i == "account" && (i + 1) <= NF) {
+          user = $(i + 1)
+          sub(/\(.*/, "", user)
+        }
+      }
+    }
+    /Active account: true/ && user != "" {
+      print user
+      exit
+    }
+  ' "$1"
+}
+
+if [ -n "$PRINT_ACTIVE_GH_USER_FILE" ]; then
+  extract_active_gh_user "$PRINT_ACTIVE_GH_USER_FILE"
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+failures=0
+warnings=0
+
+doctor_fail() {
+  err "codex-doctor: $*"
+  failures=$((failures + 1))
+}
+
+doctor_warn() {
+  warn "codex-doctor: $*"
+  warnings=$((warnings + 1))
+}
+
+doctor_ok() {
+  ok "codex-doctor: $*"
+}
+
+run_capture() {
+  local out rc
+  out="$("$@" 2>&1)"
+  rc=$?
+  printf '%s\n' "$out"
+  return "$rc"
+}
+
+check_tool() {
+  local tool="$1"
+  if command -v "$tool" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    doctor_ok "tool found: $tool ($(command -v "$tool"))"
+  else
+    doctor_fail "missing tool: $tool"
+  fi
+}
+
+info "codex-doctor: repo root $REPO_ROOT"
+
+if [ ! -d "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
+  doctor_fail "repo root does not exist: $REPO_ROOT"
+elif [ ! -e "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ] && ! git -C "$REPO_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  doctor_fail "not a git checkout: $REPO_ROOT"
+fi
+
+case "$REPO_ROOT" in
+  "$EXPECTED_ZOOCLAW_ROOT"/*|"$EXPECTED_ZOOCLAW_ROOT")
+    doctor_ok "repo root is under the Zooclaw workspace"
+    ;;
+  *)
+    doctor_warn "repo root is outside $EXPECTED_ZOOCLAW_ROOT; srpone account/path assumptions may not apply."
+    ;;
+esac
+
+info "codex-doctor: repo harness"
+for f in \
+  scripts/verify-web.sh \
+  scripts/verify-py.sh \
+  scripts/dev-mock.sh \
+  scripts/dev-staging.sh \
+  scripts/watch-pr-checks.sh \
+  scripts/open-chrome-debug-profile.sh \
+  scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh; do
+  if [ -x "$REPO_ROOT/$f" ] || [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/$f" ]; then
+    doctor_ok "harness present: $f"
+  else
+    doctor_fail "missing repo harness: $f"
+  fi
+done
+
+check_tool jq
+check_tool node
+check_tool python3
+[ "$SKIP_GIT" = "1" ] || check_tool git
+[ "$SKIP_NETWORK" = "1" ] || check_tool gh
+if [ "$SKIP_PNPM" = "0" ]; then
+  check_tool pnpm
+  check_tool uv
+fi
+
+if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  node_major_raw="$(NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 node -p 'process.versions.node.split(".")[0]' 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  node_major="$(printf '%s' "$node_major_raw" | tr -cd '0-9' | head -c 3)"
+  [ -n "$node_major" ] || node_major=0
+  if [ "$node_major" -ge 24 ] 2>/dev/null; then
+    doctor_ok "Node version is compatible ($(node --version))"
+  else
+    doctor_fail "Node 24+ is required by repo docs; current version is $(node --version 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
+  fi
+fi
+
+if [ "$SKIP_GIT" = "0" ] && command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -d "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
+  info "codex-doctor: git"
+  git_status="$(run_capture git -C "$REPO_ROOT" status --short --branch)"
+  rc=$?
+  if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
+    doctor_ok "git status works"
+  else
+    doctor_fail "git status failed: $(printf '%s' "$git_status" | head -1)"
+    if printf '%s' "$git_status" | grep -q '\.gitconfig.*Operation not permitted'; then
+      doctor_warn "Codex profile likely needs audited read access to ~/.gitconfig, or a sanitized Git config path."
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  remote_output="$(git -C "$REPO_ROOT" remote -v 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if printf '%s\n' "$remote_output" | grep -Eq 'https://[^[:space:]]*(@|x-access-token:|gh[pousr]_)'; then
+    doctor_fail "git remote appears to contain embedded credentials; replace with clean HTTPS/SSH plus credential helper."
+  elif [ -n "$remote_output" ]; then
+    doctor_ok "git remotes do not expose obvious credentials"
+  fi
+
+  usr_git="$(run_capture /usr/bin/git --version)"
+  rc=$?
+  if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
+    doctor_ok "/usr/bin/git works"
+  else
+    doctor_warn "/usr/bin/git failed: $(printf '%s' "$usr_git" | head -1)"
+    if printf '%s' "$usr_git" | grep -q 'invalid active developer path'; then
+      doctor_warn "Fix local Command Line Tools with xcode-select / reinstall CLT; this is local-only, not PR code."
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  if [ -x /opt/homebrew/bin/git ]; then
+    brew_git="$(run_capture /opt/homebrew/bin/git --version)"
+    rc=$?
+    if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
+      doctor_ok "/opt/homebrew/bin/git works"
+    else
+      doctor_warn "/opt/homebrew/bin/git failed: $(printf '%s' "$brew_git" | head -1)"
+    fi
+  else
+    doctor_warn "/opt/homebrew/bin/git is not executable; install Homebrew Git or rely on a fixed /usr/bin/git."
+  fi
+fi
+
+if [ "$SKIP_PNPM" = "0" ] && command -v pnpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  info "codex-doctor: pnpm"
+  pnpm_output="$(run_capture pnpm --version)"
+  rc=$?
+  if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
+    if printf '%s' "$pnpm_output" | grep -q '\.npmrc.*operation not permitted\|\.npmrc.*EPERM'; then
+      doctor_warn "pnpm works but cannot read ~/.npmrc. Use a sanitized NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG or audit before granting sandbox read."
+    else
+      doctor_ok "pnpm works"
+    fi
+  else
+    doctor_fail "pnpm failed: $(printf '%s' "$pnpm_output" | head -1)"
+  fi
+fi
+
+if [ "$SKIP_NETWORK" = "0" ] && command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  info "codex-doctor: gh auth"
+  gh_output="$(run_capture gh auth status)"
+  rc=$?
+  if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
+    gh_auth_tmp="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/codex-doctor-gh-auth.XXXXXX")"
+    printf '%s\n' "$gh_output" >"$gh_auth_tmp"
+    active_gh_user="$(extract_active_gh_user "$gh_auth_tmp" || true)"
+    rm -f "$gh_auth_tmp"
+    if [ -z "$EXPECTED_GH_USER" ]; then
+      doctor_ok "gh active account is ${active_gh_user:-unknown}; set CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER to enforce an expected srpone account."
+    elif [ "$active_gh_user" = "$EXPECTED_GH_USER" ]; then
+      doctor_ok "gh active account is $EXPECTED_GH_USER"
+    else
+      doctor_warn "gh auth works, but active account is ${active_gh_user:-unknown}; srpone work may need gh auth switch --user $EXPECTED_GH_USER."
+    fi
+  else
+    doctor_warn "gh auth status failed: $(printf '%s' "$gh_output" | head -1)"
+  fi
+fi
+
+if [ "$SKIP_CODEX_CONFIG" = "0" ]; then
+  info "codex-doctor: Codex config"
+  if [ -r "$CODEX_CONFIG" ]; then
+    if grep -q 'default_permissions = "ecap-local-browser"' "$CODEX_CONFIG"; then
+      doctor_ok "active Codex permission profile is ecap-local-browser"
+    else
+      doctor_warn "Codex config does not show default_permissions = ecap-local-browser"
+    fi
+    grep -q "$EXPECTED_CODEX_SESSIONS" "$CODEX_CONFIG" \
+      && doctor_ok "Codex sessions path is readable in config" \
+      || doctor_warn "Codex sessions path not found in config"
+    grep -q "$EXPECTED_CODEX_PLUGIN_CACHE" "$CODEX_CONFIG" \
+      && doctor_ok "Codex plugin cache path is readable in config" \
+      || doctor_warn "Codex plugin cache path not found in config"
+  else
+    doctor_warn "cannot read Codex config: $CODEX_CONFIG"
+  fi
+fi
+
+if [ "$failures" -gt 0 ]; then
+  err "codex-doctor: FAILED with $failures failure(s), $warnings warning(s)"
+  exit 1
+fi
+if [ "$STRICT" = "1" ] && [ "$warnings" -gt 0 ]; then
+  err "codex-doctor: STRICT failed with $warnings warning(s)"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+ok "codex-doctor: ok ($warnings warning(s))"

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+cd "$repo_root"
+
+tmpdir="$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/codex-harness-test.XXXXXX")"
+trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
+
+fail() {
+  printf 'codex-harness-test: %s\n' "$*" >&2
+  exit 1
+}
+
+assert_contains() {
+  local haystack="$1"
+  local needle="$2"
+  [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]] || fail "expected output to contain: $needle"
+}
+
+for script in scripts/codex-doctor.sh scripts/gh-body-file.sh scripts/verify-local.sh; do
+  [ -x "$script" ] || fail "missing executable $script"
+done
+
+body="$tmpdir/body.md"
+cat >"$body" <<'BODY'
+Review update with `backticks`, shell $vars, and <angle> markers.
+BODY
+
+dry_run="$(bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh pr-comment \
+  --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace \
+  --pr 123 \
+  --body-file "$body" \
+  --dry-run)"
+assert_contains "$dry_run" "gh pr comment 123"
+assert_contains "$dry_run" "--repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace"
+assert_contains "$dry_run" "--body-file"
+[[ "$dry_run" != *"Review update with"* ]] || fail "dry-run leaked body contents instead of using a file"
+
+if bash scripts/gh-body-file.sh pr-comment --repo SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace --pr 123 --body 'inline `bad`' --dry-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  fail "gh-body-file accepted inline body text"
+fi
+
+doctor_output="$(bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --no-network --skip-git --skip-pnpm --skip-codex-config)"
+assert_contains "$doctor_output" "codex-doctor:"
+assert_contains "$doctor_output" "repo harness"
+
+gh_auth_log="$tmpdir/gh-auth.txt"
+cat >"$gh_auth_log" <<'LOG'
+github.com
+  ✓ Logged in to github.com account xuwenhao (keyring)
+  - Active account: true
+
+  ✓ Logged in to github.com account chris-srp (keyring)
+  - Active account: false
+LOG
+active_gh_user="$(bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --print-active-gh-user-from-file "$gh_auth_log")"
+[ "$active_gh_user" = "xuwenhao" ] || fail "codex-doctor parsed wrong active gh user: $active_gh_user"
+
+fakebin="$tmpdir/bin"
+mkdir -p "$fakebin"
+cat >"$fakebin/gh" <<'GH'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+if [ "$1" = "auth" ] && [ "$2" = "status" ]; then
+  cat <<'LOG'
+github.com
+  ✓ Logged in to github.com account xuwenhao (keyring)
+  - Active account: true
+
+  ✓ Logged in to github.com account chris-srp (keyring)
+  - Active account: false
+LOG
+  exit 0
+fi
+exit 2
+GH
+chmod +x "$fakebin/gh"
+doctor_default_output="$(PATH="$fakebin:$PATH" bash scripts/codex-doctor.sh --skip-git --skip-pnpm --skip-codex-config)"
+assert_contains "$doctor_default_output" "CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER"
+[[ "$doctor_default_output" != *"switch --user chris-srp"* ]] || fail "codex-doctor hard-coded chris-srp without CODEX_DOCTOR_GH_USER"
+
+verify_help="$(bash scripts/verify-local.sh --help)"
+assert_contains "$verify_help" "--changed"
+assert_contains "$verify_help" "--web-static"
+assert_contains "$verify_help" "--mock-smoke"
+
+stack_log="$tmpdir/dev-stack.log"
+cat >"$stack_log" <<'LOG'
+✓ Mock backend ready on http://localhost:8000
+✓ Dev server ready: http://localhost:3007
+LOG
+dev_url="$(bash scripts/verify-local.sh --print-dev-url-from-log "$stack_log")"
+[ "$dev_url" = "http://localhost:3007" ] || fail "verify-local parsed wrong dev URL: $dev_url"
+
+set +e
+bash scripts/verify-local.sh --self-test-step-rc 3 >/dev/null 2>&1
+verify_local_rc=$?
+set -e
+[ "$verify_local_rc" -eq 3 ] || fail "verify-local did not preserve degraded exit code 3"
+
+printf 'codex-harness-test: ok\n'

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# gh-body-file.sh — safe wrappers for GitHub writes that carry Markdown bodies.
+# The important invariant is simple: body content comes from a file, never from
+# an inline shell string where backticks / $vars can be interpreted by the shell.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+Usage:
+  gh-body-file.sh pr-comment --repo OWNER/REPO --pr N --body-file FILE [--dry-run]
+  gh-body-file.sh pr-edit    --repo OWNER/REPO --pr N --body-file FILE [--dry-run]
+  gh-body-file.sh issue-create --repo OWNER/REPO --title TITLE --body-file FILE [--dry-run]
+  gh-body-file.sh issue-edit --repo OWNER/REPO --issue N --body-file FILE [--dry-run]
+  gh-body-file.sh issue-comment-patch --repo OWNER/REPO --comment-id ID --body-file FILE [--dry-run]
+
+Refuses inline --body / -f body=... arguments. Use this for Markdown containing
+backticks, shell variables, angle brackets, or user-generated text.
+EOF
+}
+
+quote_cmd() {
+  local out="" arg
+  for arg in "$@"; do
+    printf -v arg '%q' "$arg"
+    out="${out}${out:+ }${arg}"
+  done
+  printf '%s\n' "$out"
+}
+
+mode="${1:-}"
+case "$mode" in
+  pr-comment|pr-edit|issue-create|issue-edit|issue-comment-patch) shift ;;
+  -h|--help|"") usage; exit 0 ;;
+  *) printf 'gh-body-file: unknown mode: %s\n' "$mode" >&2; usage >&2; exit 2 ;;
+esac
+
+repo=""
+pr=""
+issue=""
+title=""
+comment_id=""
+body_file=""
+dry_run=0
+
+while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --repo) repo="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --pr) pr="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --issue) issue="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --title) title="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --comment-id) comment_id="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --body-file|-F) body_file="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --dry-run) dry_run=1; shift ;;
+    --body|-b)
+      printf 'gh-body-file: inline body text is not allowed; use --body-file FILE\n' >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+    -f|--field|-Fbody|--raw-field)
+      printf 'gh-body-file: raw gh field arguments are not allowed here; use --body-file FILE\n' >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+    body=*|-fbody=*|-Fbody=*)
+      printf 'gh-body-file: inline body field is not allowed; use --body-file FILE\n' >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+    -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+    *)
+      printf 'gh-body-file: unexpected argument: %s\n' "$1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+[ -n "$repo" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --repo is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+[ -n "$body_file" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --body-file is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+[ -f "$body_file" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: body file not found: %s\n' "$body_file" >&2; exit 2; }
+
+case "$mode" in
+  pr-comment)
+    [ -n "$pr" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --pr is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+    cmd=(gh pr comment "$pr" --repo "$repo" --body-file "$body_file")
+    ;;
+  pr-edit)
+    [ -n "$pr" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --pr is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+    cmd=(gh api "repos/$repo/pulls/$pr" -X PATCH -F "body=@$body_file")
+    ;;
+  issue-create)
+    [ -n "$title" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --title is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+    cmd=(gh issue create --repo "$repo" --title "$title" --body-file "$body_file")
+    ;;
+  issue-edit)
+    [ -n "$issue" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --issue is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+    cmd=(gh issue edit "$issue" --repo "$repo" --body-file "$body_file")
+    ;;
+  issue-comment-patch)
+    [ -n "$comment_id" ] || { printf 'gh-body-file: --comment-id is required\n' >&2; exit 2; }
+    cmd=(gh api "repos/$repo/issues/comments/$comment_id" -X PATCH -F "body=@$body_file")
+    ;;
+esac
+
+if [ "$dry_run" = "1" ]; then
+  quote_cmd "${cmd[@]}"
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+exec "${cmd[@]}"

@@ -0,0 +1,176 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# verify-local.sh — one local entrypoint for static checks and optional browser
+# smokes. It composes the repo's focused scripts instead of reimplementing them.
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+WAIT_SECS=180
+RUN_CHANGED=0
+RUN_WEB=0
+RUN_PY=0
+RUN_MOCK=0
+RUN_STAGING=0
+PRINT_DEV_URL_LOG=""
+SELF_TEST_STEP_RC=""
+declare -a TARGETS=()
+
+# shellcheck source=scripts/lib/log.sh
+source "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/lib/log.sh"
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<EOF
+Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS] [TARGET...]
+
+Local verification orchestrator. Static modes return after running checks.
+Smoke modes start the dev stack, wait for the printed URL, curl /en, then stop.
+
+Options:
+  --changed          Run scripts/verify-changed.sh
+  --web-static       Run scripts/verify-web.sh [TARGET...]
+  --py-static        Run scripts/verify-py.sh
+  --mock-smoke       Start scripts/dev-mock.sh and curl the local /en route
+  --staging-smoke    Start scripts/dev-staging.sh and curl the local /en route
+  --wait SECS        Max wait for smoke URL/readiness (default: ${WAIT_SECS})
+  -h, --help         Show this message
+
+With no mode, defaults to --changed.
+EOF
+}
+
+while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --changed) RUN_CHANGED=1; shift ;;
+    --web-static) RUN_WEB=1; shift ;;
+    --py-static) RUN_PY=1; shift ;;
+    --mock-smoke) RUN_MOCK=1; shift ;;
+    --staging-smoke) RUN_STAGING=1; shift ;;
+    --wait) WAIT_SECS="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --print-dev-url-from-log) PRINT_DEV_URL_LOG="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
+    --self-test-step-rc)
+      SELF_TEST_STEP_RC="${2:-}"
+      [[ "$SELF_TEST_STEP_RC" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || { err "--self-test-step-rc requires a numeric exit code"; exit 2; }
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+    --*) err "Unknown option: $1"; usage >&2; exit 2 ;;
+    *) TARGETS+=("$1"); shift ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+extract_dev_url() {
+  sed -nE 's/.*Dev server.*ready: (http:\/\/localhost:[0-9]+).*/\1/p' "$1" | head -1
+}
+
+if [ -n "$PRINT_DEV_URL_LOG" ]; then
+  extract_dev_url "$PRINT_DEV_URL_LOG"
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+if [ "$RUN_CHANGED$RUN_WEB$RUN_PY$RUN_MOCK$RUN_STAGING" = "00000" ] && [ -z "$SELF_TEST_STEP_RC" ]; then
+  RUN_CHANGED=1
+fi
+
+failed=0
+skipped=0
+children=()
+logs=()
+
+cleanup() {
+  [ "${#children[@]}" -eq 0 ] && return
+  local pid
+  for pid in "${children[@]}"; do
+    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
+  done
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM
+
+run_step() {
+  local label="$1"; shift
+  info "$label: $*"
+  "$@"
+  local rc=$?
+  case "$rc" in
+    0)
+      ok "$label passed"
+      ;;
+    3)
+      warn "$label passed with skipped tooling (exit 3)"
+      skipped=1
+      ;;
+    *)
+      err "$label failed (exit $rc)"
+      failed=1
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+smoke_stack() {
+  local label="$1"
+  local script="$2"
+  local log
+  log="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/verify-local-${label}.XXXXXX.log")" || return 1
+  logs+=("$log")
+
+  info "$label: starting $script (log: $log)"
+  bash "$REPO_ROOT/$script" --wait "$WAIT_SECS" >"$log" 2>&1 &
+  local pid=$!
+  children+=("$pid")
+
+  local waited=0 url=""
+  while [ "$waited" -lt "$WAIT_SECS" ]; do
+    if ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
+      err "$label: stack exited before reporting a URL"
+      sed -n '1,160p' "$log" >&2
+      return 1
+    fi
+    url="$(extract_dev_url "$log" || true)"
+    if [ -n "$url" ]; then
+      break
+    fi
+    sleep 1
+    waited=$((waited + 1))
+  done
+  if [ -z "$url" ]; then
+    err "$label: no dev URL reported within ${WAIT_SECS}s"
+    sed -n '1,160p' "$log" >&2
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  info "$label: warming ${url}/en"
+  waited=0
+  while [ "$waited" -lt "$WAIT_SECS" ]; do
+    code="$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 10 "${url%/}/en" || echo 000)"
+    if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
+      ok "$label smoke passed: ${url}/en returned 200"
+      return 0
+    fi
+    sleep 2
+    waited=$((waited + 2))
+  done
+  err "$label smoke failed: ${url}/en did not return 200 within ${WAIT_SECS}s"
+  sed -n '1,200p' "$log" >&2
+  return 1
+}
+
+[ "$RUN_CHANGED" = "1" ] && run_step "changed-surface" bash "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/verify-changed.sh"
+[ "$RUN_WEB" = "1" ] && run_step "web-static" bash "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/verify-web.sh" "${TARGETS[@]}"
+[ "$RUN_PY" = "1" ] && run_step "py-static" bash "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/verify-py.sh"
+[ -n "$SELF_TEST_STEP_RC" ] && run_step "self-test" bash -c "exit $SELF_TEST_STEP_RC"
+if [ "$RUN_MOCK" = "1" ]; then
+  smoke_stack "mock" "scripts/dev-mock.sh" || failed=1
+fi
+if [ "$RUN_STAGING" = "1" ]; then
+  smoke_stack "staging" "scripts/dev-staging.sh" || failed=1
+fi
+
+if [ "$failed" = "1" ]; then
+  err "verify-local: one or more checks failed"
+  exit 1
+fi
+if [ "$skipped" = "1" ]; then
+  warn "verify-local: selected checks passed, but some tooling was skipped"
+  exit 3
+fi
+
+ok "verify-local: all selected checks passed"

message:
fix(pack-store): require current org admin for reviews (#2441)

## Summary

- Auto-approve pack submissions when the submitter belongs to the target
org and is an org admin.
- Replace Pack Store team-org dependencies with current-org login and
path-org matching.
- Require current-org admin for approve, reject, review, and deprecate
routes.

author bill-srp <bill@srp.one> 1781497252 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1781497252 +0000

fix(pack-store): require current org admin for reviews (#2441)

## Summary

- Auto-approve pack submissions when the submitter belongs to the target
org and is an org admin.
- Replace Pack Store team-org dependencies with current-org login and
path-org matching.
- Require current-org admin for approve, reject, review, and deprecate
routes.

@@ -80,3 +80,22 @@ async def get_current_org(
     if membership.status != "active":
         raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Account is suspended in this organization")
     return membership
+
+
+def require_matching_current_org(
+    org_id: str,
+    current_org: AccountOrg = Depends(get_current_org),
+) -> AccountOrg:
+    """Resolve the caller's active org and reject mismatched ``/orgs/{org_id}`` paths."""
+    if current_org.org_id != org_id:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Organization scope mismatch")
+    return current_org
+
+
+def require_current_org_admin(
+    current_org: AccountOrg = Depends(require_matching_current_org),
+) -> AccountOrg:
+    """Resolve the caller's active org and require admin role within that org."""
+    if current_org.role != "admin":
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Org admin role required")
+    return current_org

+
+    for route in pack_routes:

+    }
+    assert admin_routes <= routes_by_key
+

+            continue
+        calls = {dep.call for dep in get_flat_dependant(route.dependant).dependencies}
+        assert require_current_org_admin in calls, f"{route.path} must require current-org admin"

+            status=None,
+            author_uid="other_user",
+            current_org=_member("admin"),
+        )
+
+    list_args = m_list.await_args
+    assert list_args is not None
+    assert list_args.kwargs["author_uid"] == "other_user"
+
+
+def test_require_matching_current_org_rejects_path_org_mismatch():
+    with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info:
+        require_matching_current_org("org_other", current_org=_member("admin", org_id="org_a"))
+
+    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 403
+    assert exc_info.value.detail == "Organization scope mismatch"
+
+
+def test_require_current_org_admin_rejects_non_admin_org_user():
+    with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info:
+        require_current_org_admin(current_org=_member("user"))
+
+    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 403
+    assert exc_info.value.detail == "Org admin role required"

author: bill-srp
title: fix(web): enable session chat sharing (#2440)
message:
fix(web): enable session chat sharing (#2440)

## Summary
- Enable the existing chat replay share flow on session-thread chat
pages.
- Extract the shared provider / selection bar / created-link dialog
wrapper into `ChatShareFlowFrame`.
- Add a session-thread regression test covering the Share button and
selection mode.

## Root cause
Session-thread chat was passing a hard-coded disabled share config to
`ChatHeader`:
`enabled: false`, `selectableCount: 0`, and a no-op `onEnter`. That made
the shared header omit the Share button even when the thread had
shareable Mattermost replies.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh --no-guards
'web/app/src/app/[locale]/(app)/(chat)/chat/components/share/ChatShareFlowFrame.tsx'
'web/app/src/app/[locale]/(app)/(chat)/chat/components/ChatBody.tsx'
'web/app/src/app/[locale]/(app)/(chat)/chat/[computerId]/[agentId]/[sessionId]/SessionThreadClient.tsx'
web/app/tests/unit/app/chat-thread/SessionThreadClient.unit.spec.tsx`

Note: full local governance guards were not included because this macOS
shell hits the existing `mapfile: command not found` compatibility issue
in `web/scripts/check-no-react-in-stores.sh`; CI remains authoritative
for those guards.

author: bill-srp
title: fix(web): enable reply in session chat (#2439)
message:
fix(web): enable reply in session chat (#2439)

## Summary
- Enable the existing /chat quote-reply flow on session chat pages.
- Wire session thread messages to the shared reply composer state and
clear quotes when switching thread roots.
- Add a regression test for session chat quote reply wiring.

## Root cause
Session chat already reused OpenClawChatSurface, but it did not pass
onQuoteReply, quotedText, or onClearQuote into the shared surface. That
left the existing Reply affordance and composer quote preview disabled
on session thread routes.

## Test plan
- [x] pnpm --dir web run lint
- [x] pnpm --dir web/app exec tsc --noEmit
- [x] pnpm --dir web run test:unit --
tests/unit/app/chat-thread/SessionThreadClient.unit.spec.tsx
tests/unit/app/chat/GenClawInput.unit.spec.tsx
tests/unit/app/chat/GenClawInput-extras.unit.spec.tsx

Note: pnpm --dir web run tsc currently fails before TypeScript with pnpm
Unknown option: if-present from the root script; web/app tsc was run
directly.
